regulations.title: IND Safety Reporting
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes requirements for sponsors to report safety information during clinical development. Defines serious adverse events, unexpected adverse reactions, and IND safety reports. Requires 15-day expedited reporting for serious unexpected suspected adverse reactions.
keyRequirements: 15-day alert reports for serious unexpected suspected adverse reactions
regulations.notes: Applies to all investigational drugs and biologics under IND

regulations.title: IND Annual Reports
regulations.summaryDescription: Requires sponsors to submit annual reports within 60 days of IND anniversary date. Reports must include summary of clinical development status, safety information summary, summary of IND safety reports, and list of subjects who died or discontinued.
keyRequirements: List of IND safety reports submitted
regulations.notes: Required for all active INDs

regulations.title: Postmarketing Reporting of Adverse Drug Experiences
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes postmarketing safety reporting requirements for approved drugs. Requires applicants to review all ADE information from any source, develop written procedures, and submit expedited and periodic reports to FDA. Defines serious and unexpected adverse drug experiences.
keyRequirements: Periodic reports (quarterly for 3 years, then annual)
Review all ADE sources (domestic, foreign, literature, studies)
Field alert reports for product quality
regulations.notes: Applies to NDA and ANDA holders for marketed drugs

regulations.title: Other Postmarketing Reports
regulations.summaryDescription: Requires NDA/ANDA holders to submit annual reports, field alert reports, and other postmarketing reports. Section (b)(2)(vii) specifically requires annual status reports on postmarketing requirements (PMRs) and commitments (PMCs).
regulations.notes: NDA/ANDA holders; includes PMR/PMC status reporting

regulations.title: Postmarketing Reporting of Adverse Experiences (Biologics)
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes postmarketing safety reporting requirements for licensed biological products. Parallel structure to 21 CFR 314.80 for drugs. For vaccines, requires submission to VAERS. Includes requirements for 15-day alert reports and periodic reporting.
regulations.notes: BLA holders; vaccines report to VAERS

regulations.title: Distribution Reports (Biologics)
regulations.summaryDescription: Requires licensed manufacturers to submit reports on the quantity of each lot distributed, including lot numbers and distribution dates. Critical for vaccine lot tracking and safety surveillance.
regulations.notes: BLA holders; vaccine lot distribution tracking

regulations.title: OTC Drug Safety Reporting
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes safety reporting requirements for OTC drug products marketed under monograph or approved NDA. Requires reporting of serious adverse events within 15 days.
regulations.notes: OTC monograph drugs and approved OTC NDAs

regulations.title: Investigator Reports to Sponsor
regulations.summaryDescription: Requires investigators to immediately report to the sponsor any adverse event that is both serious and unexpected, whether or not considered drug-related. Investigators must also report any event required to be reported by IRB.
keyRequirements: Immediate reporting of serious unexpected AEs to sponsor
Provide all safety information requested by sponsor
regulations.notes: Clinical trial investigators

regulations.title: BA/BE Study Safety Reporting
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes safety reporting requirements for bioavailability and bioequivalence studies that are exempt from IND requirements. Requires reporting of serious adverse events.
keyRequirements: Report serious AEs from BA/BE studies
regulations.notes: BA/BE studies exempt from IND requirements

regulations.title: Biological Product Deviation Reporting
regulations.summaryDescription: Requires reporting of biological product deviations that may affect safety, purity, or potency of distributed products. Includes manufacturing, testing, packaging, labeling, storage, and distribution deviations.
keyRequirements: Identify and investigate all manufacturing deviations
regulations.notes: Manufacturing deviations for distributed biologics

regulations.title: Postmarketing Studies and Clinical Trials (FDAAA)
regulations.summaryDescription: Authorizes FDA to require postmarketing studies or clinical trials to assess known serious risks, assess signals of serious risk, or identify unexpected serious risks. Establishes PMR/PMC framework. Requires status reporting and provides enforcement authority for non-compliance.
keyRequirements: FDA may require PMRs at approval or post-approval
regulations.notes: Rx drugs and biologics; authorizes PMRs

regulations.title: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategies (REMS)
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes FDA authority to require REMS to ensure benefits outweigh risks. REMS may include Medication Guide, communication plan, and Elements to Assure Safe Use (ETASU). Requires periodic assessments and allows modifications.
keyRequirements: ETASU (prescriber certification, pharmacy certification, patient enrollment)
regulations.notes: Drugs and biologics with serious safety concerns

regulations.title: Reports of Postmarketing Studies
regulations.summaryDescription: Requires applicants to report annually on the status of postmarketing commitments. Mandates FDA to track PMCs and publish annual reports in Federal Register on compliance status.
keyRequirements: Status categories (pending, ongoing, submitted, completed, released)
regulations.notes: All PMRs and 506B-reportable PMCs

regulations.title: Pediatric Research Equity Act
regulations.summaryDescription: Requires pediatric assessments for new drugs and biologics unless waived or deferred. Applicants must assess safety and effectiveness in relevant pediatric populations. Creates PREA PMRs.
regulations.notes: New drugs/biologics; pediatric safety and efficacy

regulations.title: National Childhood Vaccine Injury Act - Reporting Requirements
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes VAERS for vaccine adverse event reporting. Mandates HCP reporting of certain vaccine adverse events listed in the Reportable Events Table. Created vaccine injury compensation program.
keyRequirements: HCPs must report events on Reportable Events Table
regulations.notes: Vaccines; establishes VAERS

regulations.title: Good Pharmacovigilance Practices and Pharmacoepidemiologic Assessment
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides recommendations on good PV practices including signal detection, case series development, pharmacoepidemiologic studies, and risk management. Describes methods for identifying and evaluating safety signals.
regulations.notes: Core PV guidance; signal detection and risk management

regulations.title: Safety Reporting Requirements for INDs and BA/BE Studies
regulations.summaryDescription: Implements 21 CFR 312.32 final rule. Clarifies definitions, when to submit expedited reports, aggregate vs individual reporting, causality assessment, and IB updates. Distinguishes Category A, B, C events.
regulations.notes: Implements 2010 IND safety reporting final rule

regulations.title: Postmarketing Studies and Clinical Trials—Implementation of Section 505(o)(3)
regulations.summaryDescription: Implements FDAAA Section 505(o)(3) PMR authority. Describes categories of studies that constitute PMRs vs PMCs, milestone requirements, status reporting, and compliance expectations.
regulations.notes: PMR/PMC development and tracking

regulations.title: Format and Content of a REMS Document
regulations.summaryDescription: Describes structure and content requirements for REMS documents. Covers REMS goals, elements (Medication Guide, communication plan, ETASU), implementation systems, timetables, and assessment requirements.
regulations.notes: Products requiring REMS

regulations.title: REMS Modifications and Revisions
regulations.summaryDescription: Distinguishes between REMS modifications (requiring FDA approval) and revisions (notification only). Describes submission requirements, review timelines, and processes for different types of REMS changes.
regulations.notes: REMS change management

regulations.title: Best Practices for FDA Staff in Postmarketing Safety Surveillance
regulations.summaryDescription: Required by 21st Century Cures Act. Describes FDA risk-based approach to postmarket safety surveillance including FAERS screening, signal detection, pharmacoepidemiologic studies, and Sentinel system use.
regulations.notes: FDA internal practices; transparency document

regulations.title: Clinical Safety Data Management: Definitions and Standards for Expedited Reporting
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes harmonized definitions for adverse events, adverse drug reactions, serious AEs, and unexpected reactions. Defines criteria for expedited reporting during clinical development. Foundation document for global PV.
keyRequirements: Serious criteria (death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomaly, medically important)
15-day expedited reporting for serious unexpected suspected ADRs
regulations.notes: Foundation for global safety reporting definitions

regulations.title: Electronic Transmission of Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs)
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines standardized data elements and XML format for electronic ICSR transmission. R3 version expanded to support pre- and post-market reporting. Enables global regulatory submission of safety reports.
regulations.notes: Current global standard for electronic ICSR transmission

regulations.title: Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report (PBRER)
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines format and content for periodic safety reports post-approval. Introduces benefit-risk evaluation framework. Harmonizes PSUR requirements globally. Includes cumulative and interval safety analysis.
regulations.notes: Replaces PSUR for ICH regions

regulations.title: Post-Approval Safety Data Management
regulations.summaryDescription: Extends E2A concepts to post-approval phase. Defines reporting sources, expedited and periodic reporting requirements, reportability criteria, and good case management practices for marketed products.
regulations.notes: Post-marketing safety reporting framework

regulations.title: Pharmacovigilance Planning
regulations.summaryDescription: Describes approach to PV planning during development and at approval. Introduces Safety Specification concept (epidemiology, identified risks, potential risks, missing information). Framework for pharmacovigilance and risk minimization activities.
regulations.notes: Safety specification and PV planning

regulations.title: Development Safety Update Report (DSUR)
regulations.summaryDescription: Standardizes annual safety reporting during clinical development. Replaces IND Annual Report safety section in harmonized regions. Provides comprehensive annual review of safety information across all trials.
regulations.notes: Annual safety report during clinical development

regulations.title: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities
regulations.summaryDescription: Standardized medical terminology dictionary for regulatory communication. Required for ICSR coding in most jurisdictions. Hierarchical structure (SOC, HLGT, HLT, PT, LLT). Updated semi-annually by MedDRA MSSO.
keyRequirements: Use current MedDRA version for coding
regulations.notes: Required terminology for AE coding

regulations.title: Community Procedures for Authorisation and Supervision of Medicinal Products
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes EMA and centralized procedure for medicinal product authorization. Defines pharmacovigilance obligations for centrally authorized products including adverse reaction reporting, PSURs, and signal management.
regulations.notes: Centrally authorized products; establishes EMA

regulations.title: Community Code Relating to Medicinal Products for Human Use
regulations.summaryDescription: Primary EU pharmaceutical legislation for nationally authorized products. Title IX (Articles 101-108) establishes pharmacovigilance requirements including QPPV, PSMF, adverse reaction reporting, and risk management.
regulations.notes: Nationally authorized products; Title IX covers PV

regulations.title: Pharmacovigilance Activities and Good Pharmacovigilance Practices
regulations.summaryDescription: Implements pharmacovigilance provisions of Directive 2001/83/EC and Regulation 726/2004. Details QPPV role, PSMF content, signal management, PSUR format, RMP requirements, and EudraVigilance procedures.
regulations.notes: Implements Title IX PV requirements

regulations.title: Clinical Trials Regulation
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes EU clinical trial authorization and safety reporting framework. Defines SUSAR reporting obligations (Articles 41-43), DSUR requirements (Article 44), and sponsor responsibilities via Clinical Trials Information System (CTIS).
keyRequirements: SUSAR reporting to EudraVigilance CT module
regulations.notes: Replaced Directive 2001/20/EC; CTIS portal

regulations.title: Pharmacovigilance Systems and their Quality Systems
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines requirements for pharmacovigilance system establishment and quality management. Details QPPV responsibilities, qualifications, and deputization. Specifies PSMF content, maintenance, and availability requirements.
regulations.notes: QPPV and PSMF requirements

regulations.title: Risk Management Systems
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines Risk Management Plan structure, content, and submission requirements. RMP Parts: Safety Specification, Pharmacovigilance Plan, Risk Minimization Measures. Required for all new centralized MAs.
regulations.notes: RMP format and content; required for new MA

regulations.title: Collection, Management and Submission of Reports of Suspected Adverse Reactions
regulations.summaryDescription: Details ICSR collection, processing, quality, and submission requirements. Covers spontaneous reports, solicited reports, literature, patient reporting, and special situations. Specifies EudraVigilance submission requirements.
regulations.notes: Core ICSR management module

regulations.title: Periodic Safety Update Report
regulations.summaryDescription: Implements ICH E2C(R2) PBRER format in EU. Details submission frequency, EU reference dates, single assessment procedure, PSUR Repository, and PRAC assessment process.
regulations.notes: PBRER/PSUR requirements and procedures

regulations.title: Signal Management
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines signal management process including detection, validation, prioritization, evaluation, and recommended actions. Covers MAH obligations, EMA signal detection from EudraVigilance, and PRAC signal assessment.
regulations.notes: Signal detection, validation, evaluation, action

regulations.title: International Drug Monitoring: The Role of National Centres
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes framework for international drug monitoring through national pharmacovigilance centres. Foundation for WHO Programme and VigiBase global ICSR database. Defines role of Uppsala Monitoring Centre (UMC).
regulations.notes: Foundation document for WHO Programme for International Drug Monitoring

regulations.title: Surveillance Manual for Adverse Events Following Immunization
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides guidance on surveillance and investigation of adverse events following immunization (AEFI). Defines AEFI categories, causality assessment methodology, and investigation procedures.
regulations.notes: Vaccine-specific PV guidance; AEFI definitions

regulations.title: International Reporting of Adverse Drug Reactions (CIOMS Form)
regulations.summaryDescription: Established standardized format for international ADR reporting (CIOMS I form). Defined minimum data elements for case reports. Foundation for ICH E2B development.
regulations.notes: Foundation for ICSR data elements; CIOMS form still used

regulations.title: Current Challenges in Pharmacovigilance: Pragmatic Approaches
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides practical guidance on PV challenges including case processing, quality management, causality assessment, and efficient resource use. Addresses spontaneous reporting limitations.
regulations.notes: Practical PV guidance; case processing standards

regulations.title: Practical Aspects of Signal Detection in Pharmacovigilance
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive guidance on signal detection methodology including statistical methods (PRR, ROR, BCPNN), data sources, signal prioritization, and evaluation. Industry standard for signal detection practices.
regulations.notes: Definitive signal detection guidance

regulations.title: Evidence Synthesis and Meta-Analysis for Drug Safety
regulations.summaryDescription: Guidance on evidence synthesis and meta-analysis specific to drug safety evaluation. Covers methodology, data sources, heterogeneity assessment, and interpretation for regulatory decision-making.
regulations.notes: Meta-analysis methodology for safety

regulations.title: Pharmacovigilance System Master File
regulations.summaryDescription: Specifies detailed content and format requirements for the Pharmacovigilance System Master File (PSMF). PSMF documents the PV system used by MAH for authorized products. Must be maintained at declared EU location and available for inspection within 7 days.
regulations.notes: PSMF content, format, and maintenance requirements

regulations.title: Pharmacovigilance Inspections
regulations.summaryDescription: Describes pharmacovigilance inspection procedures conducted by NCAs and EMA. Covers inspection types (routine, for-cause, pre-authorization), scope, findings classification (critical, major, minor), CAPA requirements, and follow-up procedures.
keyRequirements: Inspector access to systems and personnel
regulations.notes: PV inspection procedures, findings classification, CAPA

regulations.title: Pharmacovigilance Audits
regulations.summaryDescription: Describes requirements for MAH pharmacovigilance audits as part of quality system. Covers audit planning, risk-based scheduling, audit conduct, findings management, CAPA tracking, and audit reporting. QPPV oversight of audit program.
keyRequirements: Scheduled audits of PV activities and contractors
regulations.notes: Internal PV audit requirements and methodology

regulations.title: Post-Authorisation Safety Studies
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines requirements for post-authorisation safety studies (PASS). Distinguishes imposed PASS (MA condition or PRAC request) from voluntary PASS. Covers study protocols, PRAC review, EU PAS Register, study conduct, and reporting requirements.
keyRequirements: Results within 12 months of data lock
regulations.notes: PASS requirements; imposed vs voluntary studies

regulations.title: Additional Monitoring
regulations.summaryDescription: Describes additional monitoring scheme for medicines subject to closer surveillance (black triangle ▼). Applies to new active substances, biosimilars, and products with specific safety obligations. Encourages enhanced HCP/patient reporting.
keyRequirements: Inclusion on EMA additional monitoring list
Review at least annually by PRAC
regulations.notes: Black triangle ▼ symbol products; enhanced reporting

regulations.title: Safety Communication
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides guidance on safety communication including Direct Healthcare Professional Communications (DHPCs), public statements, and crisis communication. Covers content review, dissemination, effectiveness measurement, and coordination between MAH and authorities.
regulations.notes: DHPC, public communications, crisis communication

regulations.title: Risk Minimisation Measures: Selection of Tools and Effectiveness Indicators
regulations.summaryDescription: Guidance on selection, implementation, and evaluation of risk minimization measures beyond routine labeling. Covers additional RMMs (aRMMs) such as educational materials, controlled access programs, pregnancy prevention programs, and effectiveness indicators.
regulations.notes: Additional risk minimization measures (aRMM); effectiveness evaluation

regulations.title: Definitions (Rev 4)
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides standardized definitions for pharmacovigilance terminology used across all GVP modules. Aligned with ICH E2A where applicable. Includes EU-specific terms (QPPV, PSMF, PRAC, etc.).
regulations.notes: Standardized PV terminology for EU

regulations.title: Vaccines for Human Use
regulations.summaryDescription: Product-specific guidance for vaccines addressing unique PV considerations including AEFI terminology, batch/lot identification, immunization error reporting, cluster detection, vaccine-specific signal detection, and pregnancy registries.
regulations.notes: Vaccine-specific PV considerations; AEFI

regulations.title: Biological Medicinal Products
regulations.summaryDescription: Product-specific guidance for biological medicinal products addressing traceability (brand name, batch number), immunogenicity monitoring, biosimilar considerations, and biological product-specific signal detection.
keyRequirements: Traceability: Brand name AND batch/lot number in all ICSRs
regulations.notes: Biologics-specific PV; traceability; immunogenicity

regulations.title: Human Medicines Regulations 2012 - Pharmacovigilance Provisions
regulations.summaryDescription: Primary UK legislation governing pharmacovigilance. Part 11 (Regulations 180-205) establishes PV system requirements, QPPV obligations, ICSR reporting, PSURs, RMPs, and signal management. Amended by Windsor Framework Regulations 2024 effective January 1, 2025, creating unified UK-wide MA system with Category 1/Category 2 product classifications.
keyRequirements: QPPV establishment in UK or EU/EEA
Emerging safety issue notification within 3 working days
regulations.notes: Core UK PV legislation; amended post-Brexit and Windsor Framework

regulations.title: Exceptions and Modifications to EU GVP for UK
regulations.summaryDescription: MHRA guidance specifying how EU GVP Modules apply in the UK with UK-specific exceptions and modifications. EU GVP Modules remain in force but with adaptations for UK regulatory context. Updated January 2025 for Windsor Framework implementation.
keyRequirements: Apply EU GVP with UK modifications
regulations.notes: UK-specific modifications to EU GVP Modules

regulations.title: MHRA Yellow Card ADR Reporting Scheme
regulations.summaryDescription: UK national system for collecting suspected ADR reports from HCPs, patients, and carers. Established 1964 post-thalidomide. Reports submitted via website, app, or paper forms. Electronic integration with NHS clinical systems per DCB1582 standard. Enhanced reporting encouraged for black triangle products.
keyRequirements: HCPs should report all serious ADRs
All ADRs for black triangle products
regulations.notes: UK national spontaneous reporting system since 1964

regulations.title: Qualified Person for Pharmacovigilance and National Contact Person Requirements
regulations.summaryDescription: UK QPPV requirements per HMR Regulation 182. UK QPPV can reside in UK or EU/EEA for Category 1 and Category 2 products. If QPPV not UK-based, National Contact Person for Pharmacovigilance (NCP/NCPP) required in UK. NCP must have access to UK PSMF and ADR reports, act as conduit for MHRA communications.
keyRequirements: UK QPPV for UK authorised products
NCP in UK if QPPV not UK-based
NCP access to PSMF and ADRs
regulations.notes: UK QPPV distinct from EU QPPV role

regulations.title: UK Pharmacovigilance System Master File Requirements
regulations.summaryDescription: UK PSMF requirements per HMR Regulation 182. Must be electronically accessible from UK site where ADR reports accessed. For MAs covering whole UK or NI-specific, PSMF may be located at EU site where main PV activities performed or where QPPV operates. Content and format equivalent to EU PSMF per GVP Module II.
keyRequirements: UK PSMF describes PV system for UK products
regulations.notes: UK PSMF may be separate from EU PSMF

regulations.title: Category 1 and Category 2 Product Classification
regulations.summaryDescription: Windsor Framework creates unified UK-wide MA system with two product categories determining PV requirements. Category 1: Products within mandatory/optional EU centralized procedure scope (fall under UK PV requirements). Category 2: All other products, subject to certain EU PV requirements including PSURs submitted to EU. PLNI and PLGB license types unified to UK-wide MA.
keyRequirements: Category 1 - UK PV requirements, PSUR to MHRA
Category 2 - EU PV requirements apply, PSUR per EURD list unless UK-only
regulations.notes: Windsor Framework effective January 1, 2025

regulations.title: Individual Case Safety Report Submission to MHRA
regulations.summaryDescription: MAH ICSR submission requirements to MHRA. All UK ICSRs (serious and non-serious from UK including NI) plus serious ICSRs from non-UK countries submitted via MHRA Gateway or ICSR Submissions Portal. For NI products, also submit to EudraVigilance per EU requirements. NI ICSRs identified by country code XI.
keyRequirements: NI dual reporting to both MHRA and EudraVigilance
regulations.notes: Dual submission to UK and EU for NI products

regulations.title: Emerging Safety Issue Notification Requirements
regulations.summaryDescription: MAH obligation to notify MHRA of emerging safety issues within 3 working days of establishing that a signal or safety issue from any source meets the emerging safety issue definition. Faster than EU timeline. MHRA collaboration encouraged on emerging signals for timely interventions.
keyRequirements: Notify MHRA within 3 working days of establishing emerging safety issue
regulations.notes: UK-specific 3 working day timeline

regulations.title: Periodic Safety Update Report Submission to MHRA
regulations.summaryDescription: UK PSUR/PBRER requirements vary by product category. Category 1: Submit PSUR/PBRER to MHRA, EU format with UK-specific content. Category 2: Generally follow EU requirements; submit to MHRA only if product not EU-authorized, not on EURD list, or parallel import. PBRER format per ICH E2C(R2).
keyRequirements: Category 1: PSUR to MHRA with UK-specific data
Category 2: Per EU requirements unless UK-only
EU format accepted with UK addendum
regulations.notes: Requirements differ by product category

regulations.title: Risk Management Plan Submission to MHRA
regulations.summaryDescription: UK RMP requirements per HMR 2012. EU RMP format accepted. MHRA may request UK-specific RMP modifications. For IRP (International Recognition Procedure) products, RMP from reference authority accepted with UK adaptations. RMP updates submitted via variations.
regulations.notes: EU RMP format accepted with UK modifications

regulations.title: Black Triangle Enhanced Monitoring in UK
regulations.summaryDescription: UK black triangle (▼) scheme for medicines subject to additional monitoring. MHRA maintains UK black triangle list (separate from EMA list for NI). New active substances, biosimilars, and products with specific safety obligations. Enhanced Yellow Card reporting encouraged for all suspected ADRs.
keyRequirements: Report ALL suspected ADRs via Yellow Card
Review for removal typically after 5 years or when safety profile established
regulations.notes: UK maintains own black triangle list post-Brexit

regulations.title: Drug Safety Update and Safety Communications
regulations.summaryDescription: MHRA publishes Drug Safety Update monthly bulletin with safety advice. MAH safety communications (DHPCs) require MHRA review. Risk information may be published on national medicines web portal. Crisis communication procedures for urgent safety issues.
keyRequirements: DHPC content and distribution plan to MHRA
Drug Safety Update subscription for emerging advice
regulations.notes: Drug Safety Update monthly bulletin

regulations.title: Post-Authorisation Safety Study Requirements for UK
regulations.summaryDescription: Post-authorisation safety study requirements for UK. MHRA may impose PASS as MA condition. EU PASS obligations pre-Brexit may continue. New UK-specific PASS per MHRA request. Protocol submission, interim and final reports required. Study results may inform UK regulatory decisions independent of EU.
keyRequirements: Results within 12 months of data lock
regulations.notes: UK PASS may differ from EU PASS obligations

regulations.title: Signal Detection and Management Requirements for UK
regulations.summaryDescription: UK signal detection and management per EU GVP Module IX with UK modifications. MAH responsible for continuous monitoring of safety data including UK-specific sources. MHRA conducts own signal detection using Yellow Card data. Emerging safety issues notified within 3 working days. MHRA publishes validated signals via Drug Safety Update.
keyRequirements: Signal detection from all sources including UK Yellow Card
Emerging safety issue notification (3 working days)
Collaboration with MHRA on signal assessment
regulations.notes: MHRA increased focus on compliance from 2025

regulations.title: MHRA Pharmacovigilance Inspections
regulations.summaryDescription: MHRA PV inspections of MAH systems and processes. Routine and for-cause inspections. Focus on UK law (Statutory Instruments) and EU GVP Modules as adopted. Findings classified per EU system (critical/major/minor). CAPA required. Increased compliance focus announced for 2025 including UK-specific PV activities.
keyRequirements: Compliance with HMR 2012 and UK-adopted GVP
regulations.notes: Increased compliance focus from January 2025

regulations.title: Requirements on Content and Format of Labeling for Human Prescription Drug and Biological Products
regulations.summaryDescription: NDA, ANDA, BLA approved products
keyRequirements: Establishes content and format requirements for prescription drug labeling (package insert/prescribing information). §201.56 specifies required sections and organization. §201.57 details specific content requirements for each section including: Highlights of Prescribing Information, Boxed Warning, Contraindications, Warnings and Precautions, Adverse Reactions, Drug Interactions, Use in Specific Populations. Key safety provisions: (1) Adverse Reactions section must list clinically significant reactions with frequency data

(2) Labeling must be revised to include warning about clinically significant hazard as soon as reasonable evidence of causal association exists - causal relationship need NOT be definitively established
(3) Warnings and Precautions must include patient monitoring recommendations critical to safe use. PLR format required for products approved after June 30, 2006. Older products follow §201.80.
regulations.notes: United States

regulations.title: Supplements and Other Changes to an Approved NDA
regulations.summaryDescription: NDA and ANDA holders
keyRequirements: Establishes mechanisms for making changes to approved NDAs, including safety-related labeling changes. Three supplement categories for labeling: (1) Prior Approval Supplement (PAS) - requires FDA approval before implementation
(2) Changes Being Effected in 30 Days (CBE-30) - may implement 30 days after submission unless FDA notifies otherwise

(3) Changes Being Effected (CBE-0) - may implement immediately upon submission. Per §314.70(c)(6)(iii), safety labeling changes to add or strengthen warnings, precautions, contraindications, or adverse reactions may be made via CBE-0 supplement - allows immediate implementation to protect public health. FDA may subsequently require modification. Section 505(o)(4) of FD&C Act provides FDA authority to require safety labeling changes.
regulations.notes: United States

regulations.officialIdentifier: 21 CFR 314.80(b); EU GVP Module VI; ICH E2D Section 3.2
regulations.title: Medical Literature Monitoring (MLM) Requirements for Pharmacovigilance
regulations.summaryDescription: All authorized medicinal products
keyRequirements: Medical literature monitoring (MLM) is a mandatory PV activity across all major jurisdictions. FDA: 21 CFR 314.80(b) requires applicants to "promptly review all adverse drug experience information...including...reports in the scientific literature and unpublished scientific papers." Serious unexpected ADEs from literature require 15-day reporting per 314.80(c)(1)(i) and must be accompanied by copy of published article per 314.80(d). EU GVP Module VI: MAHs must monitor worldwide scientific and medical literature for ICSRs

EMA performs MLM service for active substances in centrally authorized products via EudraVigilance. ICH E2D: Sponsor should review scientific literature for safety information as part of ongoing safety surveillance. UK MHRA: Follows EU GVP Module VI requirements with UK-specific adaptations.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions)

regulations.title: Good Clinical Practice: Integrated Addendum to ICH E6(R1)
regulations.summaryDescription: All clinical trials of medicinal products
keyRequirements: International ethical and scientific quality standard for designing, conducting, recording and reporting clinical trials involving human subjects. Contains critical safety reporting obligations: (1) Investigator must report all serious adverse events (SAEs) immediately to sponsor except those identified in protocol as not requiring immediate reporting
(2) Sponsor must ensure ongoing safety evaluation of investigational product
(3) Sponsor must notify investigators and regulatory authorities of findings that could adversely affect subject safety
(4) Investigator must report SAEs to IRB/IEC per local requirements. Section 4.11 details investigator safety reporting
Section 5.17 details sponsor safety reporting obligations. ICH E6(R3) draft released for principles-based modernization.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - SUPERSEDED: E6(R3) Step 4 adopted 06 January 2025. EMA effective 23 July 2025. FDA finalized 09 September 2025. Transition period applies.

regulations.title: Clinical Holds and Requests for Modification
regulations.summaryDescription: All INDs and IND-exempt studies
keyRequirements: Establishes FDA authority to place clinical investigations on hold (full or partial) when safety concerns arise. A clinical hold is an order issued by FDA to the sponsor to delay a proposed clinical investigation or to suspend an ongoing investigation. Grounds for clinical hold include: (1) Human subjects would be exposed to unreasonable and significant risk of illness or injury
(2) Investigator brochure is misleading, erroneous, or materially incomplete
(3) The plan or protocol is deficient in design to meet stated objectives

(4) For Phase 1, insufficient information to assess risks. Sponsor must notify all investigators of clinical hold and ensure no new subjects enrolled. To resume, sponsor must respond to FDA with complete response addressing all issues. Key safety mechanism allowing FDA to halt trials when emerging safety signals warrant.
regulations.notes: United States

regulations.title: GVP Module XVI Addendum I: Risk Minimisation Measures for Medicinal Products with Embryo-Fetal Risks (Pregnancy Prevention Programmes)
GVP Chapter P.III: Pharmacovigilance for Pregnant and Breastfeeding Women
GVP Chapter P.IV: Product- or Population-Specific Considerations IV: Paediatric Population
Concerning the Examination of Variations to the Terms of Marketing Authorisations for Medicinal Products for Human Use
General Considerations for Clinical Studies
Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials: Addendum on Estimands and Sensitivity Analysis
General Principles for Planning and Design of Multi-Regional Clinical Trials
Data Integrity and Compliance With Drug CGMP: Questions and Answers

regulations.officialIdentifier: MHRA GXP Data Integrity Guidance and Definitions
regulations.title: GXP Data Integrity Guidance and Definitions; Revision 1
Review of Ongoing Investigations - Sponsor Oversight
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes mandatory sponsor oversight obligations for ongoing clinical investigations. Sponsors must monitor investigations, ensure protocol compliance, secure investigator compliance with regulations, and review/evaluate evidence relating to safety and effectiveness. Critical provision: Sponsor must promptly review all information relevant to the safety of the drug or to the evaluation of the drug, including from foreign sources. Requires sponsor to discontinue or modify investigations if warranted by safety findings.
keyRequirements:
1. SPONSOR MONITORING OBLIGATIONS:
   - Monitor the progress of all clinical investigations conducted under IND
   - Secure compliance of investigators with protocol and regulations
   - Ensure proper conduct of clinical investigations

2. SAFETY REVIEW REQUIREMENTS:
   - Review and evaluate evidence relating to safety of the drug
   - Review and evaluate evidence relating to effectiveness
   - Promptly review ALL information relevant to drug safety
   - Include safety information from ANY source (foreign and domestic)

3. ACTION REQUIREMENTS:
   - Discontinue investigations presenting unreasonable risk
   - Modify investigations as necessary based on safety review
   - Notify FDA and investigators of significant safety findings

4. DOCUMENTATION:
   - Maintain adequate records of monitoring activities
   - Document safety reviews and decisions made
regulations.notes: United States - All IND sponsors

regulations.title: Institutional Review Boards - Safety Reporting Requirements
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes IRB requirements for clinical research including safety reporting. Key PV section: 56.108(b) requires procedures for ensuring prompt reporting to the IRB of unanticipated problems involving risks to subjects or others, including adverse events. IRB must have procedures for reporting such problems to FDA and sponsor. 56.109 requires IRB review at intervals appropriate to degree of risk, but not less than annually. IRBs serve as critical safety oversight for clinical trials.
keyRequirements:
1. IRB FUNCTIONS - 56.108:
   (a) Follow written procedures for initial and continuing review
   (b) ENSURE PROMPT REPORTING of unanticipated problems involving risks
   (b)(1) Including adverse events that may affect safety or alter risk/benefit
   (b)(2) Reporting to IRB, appropriate officials, FDA, and sponsor

2. IRB REVIEW CRITERIA - 56.111:
   - Risks minimized through sound research design
   - Risks reasonable in relation to anticipated benefits
   - Adequate provisions for monitoring data collected to ensure safety
   - Adequate provisions to protect privacy of subjects

3. CONTINUING REVIEW - 56.109:
   - Review at intervals appropriate to degree of risk
   - Not less than once per year
   - Authority to observe consent process and research conduct

4. SUSPENSION/TERMINATION - 56.113:
   - IRB may suspend or terminate research not conducted per requirements
   - May suspend if research associated with unexpected serious harm
   - Must report reasons to investigator, institution, and FDA
regulations.notes: United States - All FDA-regulated clinical research

regulations.title: Protection of Human Subjects - Informed Consent and Risk Disclosure
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes requirements for informed consent in FDA-regulated research, including mandatory disclosure of safety information. Key PV section: 50.25(a)(2) requires description of reasonably foreseeable risks or discomforts to the subject. 50.25(b)(5) requires disclosure of significant new findings developed during research that may relate to willingness to continue participation. This creates ongoing obligation to communicate emerging safety signals to enrolled subjects.
keyRequirements:
1. BASIC ELEMENTS OF INFORMED CONSENT - 50.25(a):
   (1) Statement that study involves research, with purposes and duration
   (2) DESCRIPTION OF REASONABLY FORESEEABLE RISKS OR DISCOMFORTS
   (3) Description of potential benefits
   (4) Disclosure of alternative procedures/treatments
   (5) Extent of confidentiality protections
   (6) Compensation and treatment availability for injury
   (7) Contact information for questions about research and rights
   (8) Statement that participation is voluntary

2. ADDITIONAL ELEMENTS - 50.25(b):
   (5) SIGNIFICANT NEW FINDINGS during research that may affect willingness to continue must be disclosed
   - This includes new safety signals, emerging adverse event patterns
   - Requires protocol amendment and re-consent when risks materially change

3. DOCUMENTATION - 50.27:
   - Written informed consent document
   - Copy provided to subject
   - IRB-approved consent form required

4. WAIVER CONDITIONS - 50.23:
   - Life-threatening situations
   - Specific FDA-approved protocols
regulations.notes: United States - All FDA-regulated clinical research involving human subjects

regulations.title: Postmarketing Studies for Approved Abbreviated New Drug Applications (ANDAs)
regulations.summaryDescription: Extends postmarketing study requirements (PMR/PMC) from 21 CFR 314.81 to abbreviated new drug applications. Requires ANDA holders to conduct postmarketing studies when FDA determines additional safety data is needed. Critical for generic drug pharmacovigilance - ensures equivalent safety monitoring obligations between brand and generic manufacturers.
keyRequirements:
1. PMR/PMC APPLICABILITY TO ANDAs:
   - 314.98 incorporates by reference 314.81(b)(2)(vii)
   - ANDA holders subject to same PMR/PMC obligations as NDA holders
   - Studies required when questions remain about drug safety in approved population

2. STUDY TYPES:
   - Clinical safety studies
   - Nonclinical safety studies
   - Observational epidemiological studies
   - Literature reviews

3. REPORTING REQUIREMENTS:
   - Annual status reports within 60 days of anniversary
   - Must report: study status, progress, reasons for delays
   - Final study reports submitted as supplements

4. FDA AUTHORITY:
   - Can require PMRs under FDAAA 505(o)(3)
   - Can require PMCs as condition of approval
   - Failure to comply may result in enforcement action

5. PUBLIC DISCLOSURE:
   - PMR/PMC status published on FDA website
   - Failure to meet commitments publicly noted
regulations.notes: United States - Generic drug products approved via ANDA

regulations.title: Annual Progress Reports of Postmarketing Studies for Licensed Biological Products
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes requirements for annual progress reports on postmarketing studies for biologics. Parallel to 21 CFR 314.81 requirements for drugs. Ensures FDA oversight of safety commitments made during BLA approval. Critical for biologics PV as many biological products have enhanced monitoring requirements.
keyRequirements:
1. ANNUAL PROGRESS REPORT REQUIREMENTS:
   - Due within 60 days of anniversary of BLA approval
   - Report on each postmarketing study commitment
   - Include: study status, enrollment, completion timeline, any protocol changes

2. STUDY STATUS CATEGORIES:
   - Pending: Study not yet initiated
   - Ongoing: Study in progress
   - Delayed: Behind schedule
   - Terminated: Study ended early
   - Submitted: Final report submitted

3. CONTENT REQUIREMENTS:
   - Commitment description
   - Schedule with milestones
   - Current status with explanation
   - Any changes to protocol or timeline
   - Reasons for delays

4. BIOLOGICS-SPECIFIC CONSIDERATIONS:
   - Immunogenicity studies often required
   - Long-term safety follow-up common
   - Manufacturing changes may trigger additional studies

5. ENFORCEMENT:
   - Failure to submit may affect future approvals
   - Public posting of compliance status
regulations.notes: United States - All licensed biological products including vaccines, blood products, biosimilars

regulations.title: Complaint Files - Current Good Manufacturing Practice
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes requirements for handling complaints related to drug product quality. Critical manufacturing/PV interface - complaints about product quality may contain adverse event information requiring PV follow-up. FDA Form 483 observations frequently cite inadequate complaint handling. Requires written procedures for complaint receipt, review, and investigation.
keyRequirements:
1. WRITTEN PROCEDURES REQUIRED:
   - Receipt and handling of all complaints
   - Review and evaluation
   - Investigation process
   - Decision criteria for investigation

2. COMPLAINT FILE CONTENTS:
   - Name and strength of product
   - Lot number
   - Name and address of complainant
   - Nature of complaint
   - Reply to complainant
   - Investigation findings and follow-up

3. INVESTIGATION REQUIREMENTS:
   - Quality control unit must review and approve/reject
   - Written record of investigation
   - If no investigation, document reason
   - Investigate all complaints involving possible product failure

4. PV INTERFACE:
   - Complaints with adverse events must be routed to PV
   - Medical review for AE identification
   - Coordinate quality investigation with safety assessment
   - Field alerts may be triggered by complaint patterns

5. RETENTION:
   - Maintain files for 1 year after expiration date or 3 years after distribution
regulations.notes: United States - All drug manufacturers subject to CGMP; Critical interface between Quality and PV

regulations.title: Responsibilities of Quality Control Unit - CGMP
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines responsibilities of the quality control unit in pharmaceutical manufacturing. Subsection (d) specifically requires procedures for production and process control deviations. Important for PV because product quality issues may impact safety profile and quality unit must coordinate with PV on safety-related quality events.
keyRequirements:
1. QUALITY UNIT AUTHORITY - 211.22(a):
   - Responsibility for approving/rejecting all procedures affecting identity, strength, quality, purity
   - Authority to review production records
   - Approve/reject drug products

2. WRITTEN PROCEDURES - 211.22(d):
   - Procedures must exist describing handling of deviations
   - Investigation and documentation required
   - Corrective action determination

3. PV INTERFACE:
   - Quality deviations may trigger safety assessment
   - Out-of-specification results may indicate stability/safety issues
   - Quality unit notifies PV of potential safety-related deviations
   - Coordinate on batch disposition decisions for safety-impacted lots

4. INSPECTION FOCUS:
   - FDA Form 483 frequently cites 211.22(d) violations
   - Lack of deviation procedures is common finding
   - Inadequate investigation thoroughness cited
regulations.notes: United States - Manufacturing facilities; Key interface with PV for product quality decisions

regulations.title: Production Record Review - Investigation of Discrepancies
regulations.summaryDescription: Requires thorough investigation of any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any component to meet specifications. One of the most frequently cited FDA Form 483 observations. Critical for PV because inadequate investigations may miss safety-impacting quality failures.
keyRequirements:
1. REVIEW REQUIREMENTS:
   - All production and control records reviewed by quality unit before batch release
   - Any unexplained discrepancy must be investigated
   - Investigation must extend to other batches that may be affected

2. INVESTIGATION SCOPE:
   - Include other batches of same product
   - Include other products potentially affected
   - Document thoroughly
   - Determine root cause

3. FAILURE INVESTIGATION:
   - Out-of-specification investigations
   - Process deviations
   - Equipment failures
   - All results (including failing) must be retained

4. PV RELEVANCE:
   - Quality failures may indicate safety issues
   - Super-potent or sub-potent batches create AE risk
   - Contamination may cause adverse reactions
   - Investigation findings inform field alert decisions

5. FDA FOCUS:
   - #1 cited CGMP violation
   - Incomplete investigations flagged
   - 'Testing into compliance' prohibited
regulations.notes: United States - Manufacturing batch record review; Investigation requirements

regulations.title: Product- or Population-Specific Considerations IV: Blood Components
regulations.summaryDescription: Product-specific guidance for pharmacovigilance of blood-derived medicinal products. Addresses unique safety concerns including transmissible agents, immunogenicity, and traceability requirements. Links PV obligations to blood/tissue directives and haemovigilance systems.
keyRequirements:
1. SCOPE:
   - Plasma-derived medicinal products (PDMPs)
   - Recombinant equivalents of plasma proteins
   - Products derived from human blood/plasma

2. SPECIAL SAFETY CONCERNS:
   - Transmissible agents (viral, prion)
   - Immunogenicity (inhibitor development)
   - Allergic/anaphylactic reactions
   - Thromboembolic events
   - Volume overload

3. TRACEABILITY:
   - Batch-level traceability mandatory
   - Link donation to final product to patient
   - Coordination with blood establishment systems

4. HAEMOVIGILANCE INTERFACE:
   - Serious adverse reactions reported to haemovigilance
   - Coordination between MAH PV and blood establishments
   - Look-back procedures when donor later found positive

5. ICSR SPECIFICS:
   - Include batch/donation information
   - Report transmissible agent investigations
   - Follow-up on implicated donations
regulations.notes: EU - Plasma-derived medicinal products and recombinant blood products

regulations.title: Electronic Standards for the Transfer of Regulatory Information (ESTRI)
regulations.summaryDescription: Umbrella framework for electronic regulatory information exchange. Encompasses eCTD (M2 EWG), ICSR transmission (ICH E2B), and related electronic standards. Foundation for global electronic PV submissions and regulatory data exchange.
keyRequirements:
1. FRAMEWORK COMPONENTS:
   - eCTD (electronic Common Technical Document)
   - ICH E2B ICSR transmission
   - IDMP (Identification of Medicinal Products)
   - Controlled vocabularies (MedDRA, UCUM, etc.)

2. SAFETY MODULE (eCTD Module 2.7):
   - Summary of clinical safety
   - Tabulation of adverse events
   - Integrated safety summary format

3. ICSR ELECTRONIC TRANSMISSION:
   - E2B(R3) XML schema
   - Regional implementation guides
   - Gateway/hub specifications

4. VERSIONING:
   - eCTD 4.0 current standard
   - E2B(R3) current ICSR standard
   - Regional adoption timelines vary

5. VALIDATION:
   - Schema validation required
   - Business rules per region
   - Acknowledgment/error handling
regulations.notes: All ICH regions - Electronic submission standards including ICSRs

regulations.title: Common Technical Document - Efficacy (including Integrated Summaries)
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines clinical documentation requirements for CTD Module 5, including the critical Integrated Summary of Safety (ISS). Establishes standardized format for presenting clinical safety data in marketing applications. Essential reference for organizing pre-approval safety information.
keyRequirements:
1. MODULE 2.7 CLINICAL SUMMARY:
   - 2.7.4 Summary of Clinical Safety
   - Exposure extent
   - Adverse events
   - Clinical laboratory evaluations
   - Vital signs
   - Special safety populations
   - Post-marketing data (if any)

2. INTEGRATED SUMMARY OF SAFETY (ISS):
   - Comprehensive safety database analysis
   - AE tables by system organ class
   - Serious adverse event listings
   - Deaths and discontinuations
   - Subgroup analyses

3. SAFETY TOPICS:
   - Common adverse events (≥5%)
   - Serious adverse events
   - Dose-response relationships
   - Drug interactions
   - Special populations (elderly, pediatric, renal/hepatic)

4. PRESENTATION:
   - Standardized table formats
   - MedDRA coding required
   - Patient narratives for deaths, SAEs
   - Line listings per event type
regulations.notes: All ICH regions - NDA/BLA/MAA clinical submission format

regulations.title: Good Clinical Practice: Principles and Annexes (Complete Revision - Final)
regulations.summaryDescription: Major revision of GCP guideline introducing principles-based approach with quality-by-design focus. Key changes: risk-based quality management as backbone of compliance; proportionality - tailoring oversight to actual trial risks; modern tools including remote monitoring and eConsent; clarity on service provider delegation. Introduces Data Governance framework replacing R2's CRF-focused approach. Integrates with ICH E8(R1) quality framework. Annex 1 covers interventional trials; Annex 2 (non-traditional trials) under development.
keyRequirements:
1. PRINCIPLES-BASED APPROACH:
   - Overarching principles guide all GCP activities
   - Risk-proportionate approach
   - Quality by design embedded
   - Fit-for-purpose documentation

2. SAFETY REPORTING UPDATES:
   - Annex 2 addresses safety reporting
   - Streamlined SUSAR reporting
   - Clearer investigator obligations
   - Integration with E2A/E2F

3. SPONSOR RESPONSIBILITIES:
   - Critical process and data identification
   - Risk-based monitoring
   - Ongoing safety evaluation
   - Prompt notification of safety findings

4. INVESTIGATOR RESPONSIBILITIES:
   - SAE reporting per protocol and regulations
   - Safety-relevant protocol deviations
   - Subject protection paramount

5. DECENTRALIZED TRIALS:
   - Remote/virtual trial elements addressed
   - Technology-enabled safety monitoring
   - Direct-to-participant drug supply considerations

6. DATA GOVERNANCE:
   - Data integrity across all sources
   - Electronic systems validation
   - Audit trail requirements
regulations.notes: All ICH regions - Step 4 adopted 06 January 2025. EMA effective 23 July 2025. FDA finalized 09 September 2025. NOW IN EFFECT. Replaces E6(R2).

regulations.title: Optimization of Safety Data Collection
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides recommendations for selective safety data collection in late-stage clinical trials when safety profile is well-characterized. Allows reduction in routine AE collection while maintaining detection of new safety signals. Balances data quality with trial efficiency.
keyRequirements:
1. APPLICABILITY CRITERIA:
   - Late-stage development (typically Phase 3+)
   - Well-characterized safety profile from earlier trials
   - Adequate safety database exists
   - Regulatory agreement recommended

2. SELECTIVE COLLECTION APPROACH:
   - Focus on SAEs, AESIs, and discontinuations
   - Pre-specify AEs NOT to collect routinely
   - Maintain collection of unexpected events
   - Document rationale in protocol

3. MAINTAINED COLLECTION:
   - All SAEs regardless of relationship
   - AEs of special interest (AESI)
   - AEs leading to discontinuation/modification
   - Drug-drug interactions of concern
   - Events in special populations

4. SAFETY MONITORING:
   - DMC/DSMB oversight continues
   - Signal detection capabilities maintained
   - Ability to expand collection if signal emerges

5. DOCUMENTATION:
   - Protocol must specify approach
   - Statistical analysis plan addresses
   - CSR documents approach and rationale
regulations.notes: ICH regions - Late-stage trials with established safety profiles

regulations.title: EudraVigilance Access Policy for Medicines for Human Use
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines stakeholder access levels to EudraVigilance database and ADR Reports Online. Critical for signal detection activities - MAHs can access Line Listings for their own products and aggregate data. Healthcare professionals and public can access ADR Reports Online (adrreports.eu). Establishes data protection requirements and permitted uses.
keyRequirements:
1. STAKEHOLDER ACCESS LEVELS:
   - EMA/NCAs: Full access to all data
   - MAHs: Access to ICSRs for own products

Limited aggregate data for other products
   - HCPs/Public: Access to ADR Reports Online (aggregated, anonymized)
   - Researchers: Academic access via formal request

2. MAH ACCESS (EVDAS):
   - Electronic Reaction Monitoring Reports (eRMRs)
   - Line Listings for own products
   - Signal detection tools
   - Aggregate analysis outputs
   - Must have EudraVigilance registration

3. ADR REPORTS ONLINE (Public):
   - Suspected side effect reports
   - Aggregated by product/reaction
   - Updated weekly
   - No individual case details

4. DATA PROTECTION:
   - Personal data protected per GDPR
   - Anonymization requirements
   - Secondary use restrictions
   - Audit trail of access

5. SIGNAL DETECTION USE:
   - MAHs expected to use for signal detection
   - Complements company database monitoring
   - Statistical tools available (PRR, ROR)
regulations.notes: EU - All MAHs and stakeholders accessing EudraVigilance data

regulations.title: Format for the Pharmacovigilance System Master File (PSMF)
regulations.summaryDescription: Specifies the mandatory format and content for the Pharmacovigilance System Master File. The PSMF is a detailed description of the PV system used by the MAH. Must be maintained at QPPV location and available for inspection. Key document for demonstrating PV system compliance.
keyRequirements:
1. PSMF STRUCTURE:
   - Section A: QPPV details and contact information
   - Section B: Organizational structure
   - Section C: Medicinal products covered
   - Section D: Quality system documentation
   - Section E: Contractual arrangements
   - Section F: IT systems description
   - Section G: Training records summary
   - Section H: Process descriptions
   - Annexes: Supporting documentation

2. MANDATORY CONTENT:
   - QPPV CV and contact details
   - Organizational chart with PV responsibilities
   - List of all authorized products by MA number
   - SOPs and work instructions inventory
   - Subcontractor list with responsibilities
   - Database and system descriptions
   - Training matrix

3. MAINTENANCE:
   - Continuous updates required
   - Version control mandatory
   - Change log maintained
   - Available within 7 days for inspection

4. LOCATION:
   - Held at QPPV location (EU/EEA)
   - Electronic format permitted
   - Backup copies required

5. INSPECTION:
   - Primary document for PV inspections
   - Must accurately reflect current system
   - Discrepancies are inspection findings
regulations.notes: EU - All MAHs with centrally or nationally authorized products

regulations.title: MAH Reporting of ICSRs to EudraVigilance - Detailed Guidance
regulations.summaryDescription: Detailed technical and procedural guidance for MAH submission of ICSRs to EudraVigilance. Complements GVP Module VI with practical implementation details. Covers data element completion, validation rules, acknowledgment handling, and special case scenarios.
keyRequirements:
1. SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS:
   - E2B(R3) XML format mandatory
   - EudraVigilance Gateway or Web Trader
   - Sender identifier registered
   - Digital certificate required

2. DATA ELEMENT GUIDANCE:
   - Mandatory vs optional fields
   - Nullification procedures
   - Amendment vs follow-up distinction
   - Worldwide unique case ID

3. CASE VALIDATION:
   - Minimum criteria for valid case
   - Business rules validation
   - Schema validation
   - Acknowledgment codes and actions

4. SPECIAL SCENARIOS:
   - Parent-child reports
   - Pregnancy and outcome
   - Lack of efficacy
   - Medication errors
   - Drug interactions
   - Off-label use

5. QUALITY REQUIREMENTS:
   - MedDRA coding accuracy
   - Narrative quality standards
   - Causality assessment documentation
   - Follow-up information handling

6. TIMELINE COMPLIANCE:
   - Day 0 determination
   - 15-day serious expedited
   - 90-day non-serious
   - Clock stop scenarios
regulations.notes: EU - Technical guidance for ICSR submission to EudraVigilance

regulations.title: Innovative Licensing and Access Pathway (ILAP)
regulations.summaryDescription: UK post-Brexit flagship regulatory pathway for innovative medicines. Provides enhanced regulatory support, rolling review, and accelerated assessment. Includes Innovation Passport designation and Target Development Profile. PV implications include enhanced monitoring and potentially conditional approval with specific safety commitments.
keyRequirements:
1. ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA:
   - Innovative medicine addressing unmet medical need
   - Potential for significant patient benefit
   - UK development or first launch intent
   - Can apply at any development stage

2. INNOVATION PASSPORT:
   - Early designation confirming eligibility
   - Access to ILAP toolkit
   - Enhanced regulatory engagement
   - Not guarantee of approval

3. TARGET DEVELOPMENT PROFILE (TDP):
   - Roadmap agreed with MHRA, NICE, SMC
   - Includes PV requirements
   - Real-world evidence expectations
   - Health technology assessment alignment

4. PV REQUIREMENTS:
   - Enhanced pharmacovigilance may be required
   - Risk management plan critical
   - Post-authorization safety studies likely
   - Black Triangle monitoring typical

5. ROLLING REVIEW:
   - Submit modules as completed
   - Continuous assessment
   - Faster overall timeline

6. POST-APPROVAL:
   - Conditional approval possible
   - Specific obligations in MA
   - Enhanced safety monitoring
   - Registry participation may be required
regulations.notes: UK - Priority pathway for innovative medicines addressing unmet need

regulations.title: Conditional Marketing Authorisation (CMA) - UK Requirements
regulations.summaryDescription: UK framework for conditional marketing authorisations granted before complete clinical data available. Based on positive benefit-risk with less comprehensive data than standard MA. Requires annual renewal and specific obligations including enhanced PV. Used for serious/life-threatening conditions with unmet need.
keyRequirements:
1. ELIGIBILITY:
   - Serious or life-threatening condition
   - Unmet medical need
   - Positive benefit-risk despite incomplete data
   - Comprehensive data can be provided post-approval
   - Public health benefit of immediate availability

2. SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS:
   - Complete ongoing/new studies
   - Submit additional data by specified dates
   - Specific PV activities required
   - Annual renewal requirement

3. PV REQUIREMENTS (Enhanced):
   - Comprehensive Risk Management Plan
   - Additional risk minimisation measures likely
   - Post-authorisation safety studies (PASS)
   - Enhanced adverse event monitoring
   - Periodic safety update reports (more frequent)
   - Black Triangle designation

4. ANNUAL RENEWAL:
   - Submit renewal application yearly
   - Demonstrate progress on specific obligations
   - Updated benefit-risk assessment
   - May convert to standard MA when data complete

5. LABELING:
   - CMA status clearly indicated
   - Black Triangle symbol
   - Limitations of data described
   - Enhanced reporting encouraged

6. CONVERSION/WITHDRAWAL:
   - Convert to full MA when conditions met
   - Withdrawal if obligations not met
   - Withdrawal if benefit-risk turns negative
regulations.notes: UK - Conditional approval with enhanced PV obligations

regulations.title: FDA Implementation Guide for E2B(R3) Electronic Transmission of ICSRs
regulations.summaryDescription: FDA-specific implementation guide for ICH E2B(R3) standard. Defines US-specific data elements, validation rules, and submission requirements for electronic ICSRs to FAERS. Mandatory for all electronic safety report submissions to FDA.
keyRequirements:
1. SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS:
   - E2B(R3) XML format
   - FDA ESG (Electronic Submissions Gateway)
   - Sender registration required
   - Digital certificate (AS2)

2. FDA-SPECIFIC ELEMENTS:
   - FDA Application Number (NDA, ANDA, BLA)
   - US Agent information if foreign
   - FDA product coding
   - US-specific reaction coding

3. VALIDATION RULES:
   - FDA business rules
   - Schema validation
   - FAERS-specific checks
   - Acknowledgment/rejection codes

4. REPORT TYPES:
   - Initial reports
   - Follow-up reports
   - Nullification reports
   - Amendment handling

5. SPECIAL SCENARIOS:
   - IND safety reports
   - NDA/BLA postmarketing
   - VAERS interface (vaccines)
   - Combination products

6. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS:
   - Message structure
   - Controlled vocabularies
   - Character encoding
   - File size limits
regulations.notes: United States - Technical specifications for FAERS submissions

regulations.title: EU Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) Implementation Guide
regulations.summaryDescription: EMA implementation guide for E2B(R3) submissions to EudraVigilance. Defines EU-specific requirements, extended data elements, and validation rules. Essential reference for all MAH ICSR submissions in EU.
keyRequirements:
1. EU-SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS:
   - EU Local Number (worldwide case ID)
   - EU MA numbers
   - EV substance/product coding
   - EU-specific causality

2. EXTENDED EU DATA ELEMENTS:
   - Parent-child linking
   - Pregnancy-specific fields
   - Device information (combinations)
   - EU clinical trial identification

3. VALIDATION RULES:
   - EudraVigilance Business Rules
   - Technical validation
   - Scientific validation
   - Acknowledgment handling

4. SUBMISSION CHANNELS:
   - EudraVigilance Gateway (EVWEB)
   - EV Web Trader (low volume)
   - B2B connection (high volume)

5. XEVMPD INTEGRATION:
   - Product dictionary alignment
   - Substance identification
   - Medicinal product matching

6. SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS:
   - Article 29 (post-referral)
   - Non-interventional studies
   - Suspected transmission of infectious agents
regulations.notes: EU - Technical specifications for EudraVigilance submissions

regulations.title: MHRA Implementation Guide for E2B(R3) ICSR Submissions
regulations.summaryDescription: UK-specific implementation guide for E2B(R3) following Brexit. Defines MHRA Gateway requirements, UK-specific elements, and validation rules. Critical for all MAH submissions to MHRA. Addresses dual-submission requirements for NI products.
keyRequirements:
1. UK-SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS:
   - MHRA Gateway registration
   - UK MA numbers (PLGB, PLNI, PL)
   - XI country code for NI
   - UK sender identifier

2. SUBMISSION CHANNELS:
   - MHRA Gateway (primary)
   - Web-based submission (low volume)
   - XML via secure transfer

3. NORTHERN IRELAND CONSIDERATIONS:
   - Category 2 products: Dual submission (MHRA + EudraVigilance)
   - XI country code usage
   - Windsor Framework implications

4. VALIDATION:
   - MHRA-specific business rules
   - Schema validation
   - Acknowledgment/rejection handling

5. TIMELINES:
   - 15 days serious domestic
   - 15 days serious foreign unexpected
   - 90 days non-serious

6. YELLOW CARD INTEGRATION:
   - Healthcare professional reports via Yellow Card
   - MAH reports via Gateway
   - Case ID coordination
regulations.notes: UK - Technical specifications for MHRA Gateway submissions

regulations.title: Adaptive Clinical Trials
regulations.summaryDescription: Guidance on adaptive clinical trial designs that allow pre-planned modifications based on accumulating data. Addresses safety considerations including data monitoring committee requirements, interim safety analyses, and safety-driven adaptations. Critical for modern trial designs including platform trials.
keyRequirements:
1. ADAPTIVE DESIGN TYPES:
   - Sample size re-estimation
   - Group sequential designs
   - Response-adaptive randomization
   - Biomarker-adaptive designs
   - Seamless phase designs
   - Platform/master protocols

2. SAFETY CONSIDERATIONS:
   - Pre-specified safety stopping rules
   - DMC charter requirements
   - Interim safety analyses
   - Safety-driven adaptations
   - Unblinded safety data handling

3. DMC REQUIREMENTS:
   - Independent safety monitoring
   - Access to unblinded data
   - Recommendation authority
   - Documentation of decisions

4. REGULATORY IMPLICATIONS:
   - Early engagement recommended
   - Statistical considerations
   - Type I error control
   - Integrity of trial

5. SAFETY REPORTING:
   - Standard SUSAR requirements apply
   - Aggregate safety reviews
   - DMC safety communications
   - Adaptation triggered by safety

6. PLATFORM TRIALS:
   - Multiple interventions
   - Shared control arm
   - Adding/dropping arms
   - Coordinated safety monitoring
regulations.notes: All ICH regions - Adaptive and platform trial designs

regulations.title: Substance, Product, Organisation and Referential (SPOR) Master Data
regulations.summaryDescription: EMA's master data management programme comprising four interconnected services: Organisations Management Service (OMS), Referentials Management Service (RMS), Substance Management Service (SMS), and Product Management Service (PMS/XEVMPD). Critical for accurate product identification in ICSRs and regulatory submissions. Supports ISO IDMP implementation.
keyRequirements:
1. ORGANISATIONS MANAGEMENT SERVICE (OMS):
   - Unique organisation identifiers (ORG-ID)
   - Location identifiers (LOC-ID)
   - MAH registration required
   - QPPV location registration
   - Used in ICSR sender/receiver identification

2. REFERENTIALS MANAGEMENT SERVICE (RMS):
   - Controlled vocabularies and code lists
   - Country codes, units, routes of administration
   - MedDRA version management
   - Standardised terminology for ICSRs

3. SUBSTANCE MANAGEMENT SERVICE (SMS):
   - Unique substance identifiers
   - ISO 11238 compliance
   - Active ingredient identification
   - Critical for ICSR product matching

4. PRODUCT MANAGEMENT SERVICE (PMS/XEVMPD):
   - Extended EudraVigilance Medicinal Product Dictionary
   - EV codes for authorised products
   - Product-substance relationships
   - ICSR product identification

5. ISO IDMP ALIGNMENT:
   - Implementing ISO 11615, 11616, 11238, 11239, 11240
   - Phased implementation timeline
   - Global data standards alignment
regulations.notes: EU - Master data management for regulatory submissions including PV

regulations.title: Good Practices for Data Management and Integrity in Regulated GMP/GDP Environments
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive guidance on data integrity for GMP/GDP environments. Adopted by 54 PIC/S member authorities. Defines ALCOA+ principles and expectations for computerized systems, audit trails, and data governance. Critical intersection with PV for quality data supporting safety assessments.
keyRequirements:
1. ALCOA+ PRINCIPLES:
   - Attributable: Who performed action and when
   - Legible: Readable and permanent
   - Contemporaneous: Recorded at time of activity
   - Original: First capture or certified copy
   - Accurate: No errors, editing documented
   - Complete: All data including repeat/reanalysis
   - Consistent: Sequence of events evident
   - Enduring: Recorded on appropriate media
   - Available: Accessible for review/audit

2. DATA GOVERNANCE:
   - Organisational accountability
   - Quality culture emphasis
   - Senior management responsibility
   - Risk-based approach

3. COMPUTERIZED SYSTEMS:
   - Validation requirements
   - Access controls
   - Audit trail configuration
   - System administration controls
   - Data backup and archiving

4. PAPER RECORDS:
   - Same integrity requirements
   - Controlled blank forms
   - Contemporaneous recording
   - No pre-dating or back-dating

5. PV RELEVANCE:
   - Quality data supports safety decisions
   - Complaint data integrity
   - Batch record accuracy for safety events
   - Manufacturing deviation documentation
regulations.notes: International - PIC/S member authorities; Manufacturing/PV data interface

regulations.title: Medical Device Reporting (MDR)
regulations.summaryDescription: FDA requirements for reporting deaths, serious injuries, and malfunctions associated with medical devices. Critical for combination products where device is primary mode of action. Parallel reporting system to drug ADR reporting with different timelines and forms.
keyRequirements:
1. REPORTABLE EVENTS:
   - Death: Device may have caused or contributed
   - Serious injury: Device may have caused or contributed
   - Malfunction: Would likely cause death or serious injury if recurred

2. REPORTING TIMELINES:
   - 30 calendar days: Standard MDR reports
   - 5 work days: Remedial action required OR FDA written request
   - Supplemental reports: When new information received

3. REPORTER CATEGORIES:
   - Manufacturers: Deaths, serious injuries, malfunctions
   - Importers: Deaths, serious injuries
   - Device user facilities: Deaths, serious injuries

4. COMBINATION PRODUCTS:
   - Primary mode of action determines lead center
   - Device PMOA: MDR applies (21 CFR 803)
   - Drug PMOA: Drug ADE reporting (21 CFR 314.80)
   - May require reporting to both CDRH and CDER

5. REPORT CONTENT:
   - Patient information
   - Device identification (UDI)
   - Event description
   - Manufacturer evaluation
   - Corrective actions taken
regulations.notes: United States - Medical devices and combination products with device primary mode of action

regulations.title: Complaint Files - Quality System Regulation (Devices)
regulations.summaryDescription: Device QSR requirements for maintaining complaint files. Parallel to drug CGMP 211.198 but with device-specific provisions. Critical for combination products to coordinate device and drug complaint handling. Requires evaluation of complaints for MDR reportability.
keyRequirements:
1. COMPLAINT HANDLING PROCEDURES:
   - Receiving, reviewing, evaluating complaints
   - Designated unit for complaint review
   - Written procedures required

2. COMPLAINT FILE CONTENT:
   - Name of device
   - Date complaint received
   - Unique device identifier (UDI) or universal product code (UPC)
   - Name, address, phone of complainant
   - Nature and details of complaint
   - Investigation dates and findings
   - Corrective action taken
   - Reply to complainant

3. MDR EVALUATION:
   - Each complaint evaluated for MDR reportability
   - Death or serious injury evaluation
   - Malfunction evaluation
   - Document decision rationale

4. INVESTIGATION REQUIREMENTS:
   - Investigate if complaint involves possible failure to meet specification
   - Investigate if complaint involves injury, death, or hazard
   - Document rationale if no investigation

5. COMBINATION PRODUCTS:
   - Coordinate with drug complaint procedures
   - Determine primary mode of action
   - Route to appropriate regulatory pathway
regulations.notes: United States - Device manufacturers; Combination product quality interface

regulations.title: Regulation of Combination Products
regulations.summaryDescription: Framework for regulation of combination products composed of drug, device, and/or biological components. Defines primary mode of action (PMOA) determination, lead FDA center assignment, and CGMP/QSR requirements. Critical for PV because safety reporting requirements depend on PMOA and lead center.
keyRequirements:
1. COMBINATION PRODUCT TYPES:
   - Single entity: Components combined physically/chemically
   - Co-packaged: Separate products in single package
   - Cross-labeled: Separate products with cross-reference

2. PRIMARY MODE OF ACTION (PMOA):
   - Drug PMOA: Lead center = CDER
   - Biologic PMOA: Lead center = CBER
   - Device PMOA: Lead center = CDRH
   - Determines applicable regulations

3. GMP REQUIREMENTS (Part 4 Subpart A):
   - Drug PMOA: Drug CGMP (21 CFR 210/211) + device QSR streamlined
   - Device PMOA: Device QSR (21 CFR 820) + drug CGMP streamlined
   - Streamlined requirements in 21 CFR 4.4

4. POSTMARKET SAFETY REPORTING (Part 4 Subpart B):
   - Drug PMOA: Report under 21 CFR 310, 312, 314, 329, 600
   - Device PMOA: Report under 21 CFR 803
   - May have obligations to multiple centers
   - Coordination between centers

5. REQUEST FOR DESIGNATION (RFD):
   - Formal process to determine assignment
   - 60-day FDA response
   - Can request reconsideration
regulations.notes: United States - All combination products (drug-device, biologic-device, drug-biologic)

regulations.title: WHO Global Individual Case Safety Report Database
regulations.summaryDescription: World's largest database of suspected adverse drug reactions, maintained by the WHO Uppsala Monitoring Centre (UMC). Contains over 30 million reports from 140+ countries. Supports global signal detection and safety monitoring. Member countries submit national reports; MAHs do not submit directly but data from regional databases flows in.
keyRequirements:
1. DATABASE SCOPE:
   - Over 30 million ICSRs
   - 140+ contributing countries
   - Growing ~3 million reports/year
   - All therapeutic areas

2. DATA SOURCES:
   - National pharmacovigilance centres
   - Not direct MAH submission
   - De-duplicated where possible
   - Various formats harmonized

3. ACCESS:
   - Full access: Member countries (NCAs)
   - VigiAccess: Public web interface (aggregated)
   - VigiLyze: Analysis tool for members
   - Academic access: By application

4. SIGNAL DETECTION:
   - UMC conducts global signal detection
   - Vigibase statistical tools
   - Disproportionality analysis
   - Signal communication to members

5. DATA QUALITY:
   - Completeness varies by country
   - Duplicates possible across regions
   - Causality not confirmed
   - Reporting rates vary
regulations.notes: International - WHO member state national pharmacovigilance centres

regulations.title: WHO National Pharmacovigilance Database System
regulations.summaryDescription: Web-based ICSR management system provided free by WHO-UMC to national pharmacovigilance centres. Enables case management, signal detection, and reporting to VigiBase. Used by 130+ countries. Important context for understanding global PV infrastructure.
keyRequirements:
1. SYSTEM CAPABILITIES:
   - ICSR entry and management
   - E2B(R3) compatible
   - Causality assessment tools
   - Signal detection features
   - Automatic VigiBase submission

2. USERS:
   - National pharmacovigilance centres
   - WHO member states
   - Regional PV centres
   - Not for MAH use

3. DATA FLOW:
   - HCPs report to national centre
   - Centre enters in VigiFlow
   - Automatic transmission to VigiBase
   - De-duplication at UMC

4. FEATURES:
   - Web-based (no installation)
   - Multi-language support
   - Customizable workflows
   - Integrated dictionaries (MedDRA, WHO-DD)

5. MAH RELEVANCE:
   - Understand national reporting pathways
   - Literature cases may appear
   - Signal detection includes VigiBase data
regulations.notes: International - National pharmacovigilance centres in WHO member states

regulations.title: Management of Safety Information from Clinical Trials
regulations.summaryDescription: CIOMS guidance on managing safety information during clinical development. Addresses practical aspects of collecting, assessing, and reporting safety data from clinical trials. Complements ICH E2A/E2F with operational recommendations. Basis for many company practices.
keyRequirements:
1. SAFETY DATA COLLECTION:
   - AE collection methodology
   - SAE identification and processing
   - Data capture systems
   - Quality standards

2. CAUSALITY ASSESSMENT:
   - Methodology for clinical trials
   - Investigator vs sponsor assessment
   - Documentation requirements
   - Handling disagreements

3. EXPECTEDNESS DETERMINATION:
   - Reference document selection
   - IB/RSI updates
   - Version control
   - Global consistency

4. EXPEDITED REPORTING:
   - SUSAR identification
   - Timeline management
   - Investigator notification
   - Blinding considerations

5. AGGREGATE SAFETY REVIEW:
   - Periodic safety reviews
   - Signal detection in trials
   - DMC interactions
   - Protocol amendments for safety
regulations.notes: International - Clinical development safety management
