keyRequirements: Prior FDA approval for certain changes
regulations.notes: Applies to all investigational drugs and biologics under IND requiring protocol changes

regulations.title: Withdrawal of IND
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes procedures for IND withdrawal by sponsor or FDA. Sponsors must notify FDA and all investigators when withdrawing IND, including safety reasons for withdrawal. FDA may withdraw IND approval for safety concerns, protocol violations, or failure to submit required reports.
keyRequirements: Sponsor notification to FDA of withdrawal
Opportunity to respond to FDA-initiated withdrawal
regulations.notes: Applies when sponsors discontinue IND or FDA withdraws approval

regulations.title: Expanded Access - General Provisions
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes criteria for expanded access (compassionate use) to investigational drugs. Requires serious or immediately life-threatening condition, no comparable alternatives, potential benefit justifies risks, and no interference with clinical development. Includes individual patient, intermediate-size, and treatment IND categories.
regulations.notes: Applies to investigational drugs for serious or life-threatening conditions outside clinical trials

regulations.title: Expanded Access - Treatment IND/Protocol
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes requirements for treatment INDs and treatment protocols providing widespread access. Drug must be being investigated in controlled trials or all trials completed. Requires sufficient safety/efficacy evidence, ongoing clinical development or marketing application submission, and sponsor commitment to safety reporting.
keyRequirements: Drug in active development or NDA/BLA submitted
regulations.notes: Applies to widespread treatment use of investigational drugs

regulations.title: Foreign Clinical Studies Not Conducted Under an IND
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes criteria for FDA acceptance of foreign clinical studies not conducted under IND. Studies must conform to GCP including ethical review and informed consent. FDA may accept studies if conducted according to GCP (ICH E6) or ethical principles of Declaration of Helsinki. Safety data from foreign studies must meet same standards as domestic studies.
keyRequirements: GCP compliance (ICH E6) or Declaration of Helsinki
regulations.notes: Applies to foreign clinical studies used to support IND, NDA, or BLA submissions

regulations.title: NDA Field Alert Reports
regulations.summaryDescription: Requires NDA/ANDA holders to submit Field Alert Reports (FARs) within 3 working days of information concerning any incident that causes the drug product or its labeling to be mistaken for, or applied to, another article, or any bacteriological contamination, significant chemical/physical/other change, deterioration, or failure to meet specifications that may affect safety or efficacy.
keyRequirements: 3 working day submission for product defects
regulations.notes: Applies to NDA and ANDA holders for product quality/safety defects

regulations.title: Accelerated Approval - Withdrawal Procedures
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes procedures for FDA withdrawal of accelerated approval. FDA may withdraw approval if confirmatory trial fails to verify clinical benefit, applicant fails to conduct required post-marketing study with due diligence, promotional materials are false/misleading, or other evidence demonstrates product is not shown to be safe/effective. Provides applicant opportunity for hearing.
regulations.notes: Applies to drugs approved under accelerated approval (Subpart H)

regulations.title: Accelerated Approval of Biological Products
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes accelerated approval pathway for biological products for serious or life-threatening conditions based on surrogate endpoints or intermediate clinical endpoints. Requires post-marketing studies to verify clinical benefit. Includes provisions for promotional material review, expedited withdrawal for failure to conduct confirmatory trials, and safety-based withdrawal procedures.
regulations.notes: Applies to biological products for serious conditions approved on surrogate endpoints

regulations.title: Good Laboratory Practice for Nonclinical Laboratory Studies
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) standards for nonclinical laboratory studies that support applications for research or marketing permits. Covers organization, personnel, facilities, equipment, testing facility operations, protocols, conduct of studies, reporting, and records. Ensures quality and integrity of safety data from animal studies, in vitro tests, and other nonclinical assessments.
regulations.notes: Applies to nonclinical safety studies supporting FDA applications

regulations.title: Written Procedures; Deviations
regulations.summaryDescription: Requires written procedures for production and process control designed to assure drug products have identity, strength, quality, and purity. Any deviation from written procedures must be recorded and justified. Deviations affecting product safety or efficacy require investigation and may trigger Field Alert Reports or adverse event reporting if product distributed.
keyRequirements: Linkage to Field Alert Reports if safety-impacting
regulations.notes: Applies to all drug product manufacturing; PV interface for quality-related safety events

regulations.title: Sampling and Testing of In-Process Materials and Drug Products
regulations.summaryDescription: Requires written procedures for in-process sampling and testing of drug products to monitor adequacy of manufacturing processes. Control procedures must be established to monitor output and validate performance. In-process specifications must be consistent with final product specifications and derived from previous acceptable process data. OOS results may indicate safety concerns requiring investigation.
regulations.notes: Applies to in-process controls ensuring product safety and quality

regulations.title: Expiration Dating
regulations.summaryDescription: Requires appropriate stability testing to determine expiration dates for drug products. Expiration dates must be related to storage conditions stated on labeling. Homeopathic products exempt unless required by monograph. Stability data must support expiration dating; expired products may pose safety risks due to degradation, reduced potency, or formation of toxic degradants.
regulations.notes: Applies to expiration dating with safety implications for degraded products

regulations.title: General Biological Products Standards
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes general standards for biological products including definitions, establishment standards, establishment inspection, and adverse experience reporting. Subpart A covers scope and definitions. Subpart B covers establishment standards including personnel, physical facilities, equipment, animals, and records. Subpart C covers establishment inspection. Subpart D references safety reporting requirements now consolidated in 600.80.
regulations.notes: Applies to all licensed biological products; establishes general safety standards

regulations.title: Prescription Drug Labeling Requirements
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes requirements for prescription drug labeling exempt from adequate directions for use requirements. Labeling must include all safety information necessary for safe and effective use including contraindications, warnings, precautions, and adverse reactions. Safety labeling changes (SLCs) may be implemented via CBE-0 supplement for newly identified safety issues. Links directly to pharmacovigilance through label updates based on post-market safety data.
keyRequirements: CBE-0 supplements for safety labeling changes
regulations.notes: Applies to prescription drug labeling including safety information requirements

regulations.title: Prescription Drug Advertising
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes requirements for prescription drug advertisements to include fair balance of safety and efficacy information. Advertisements must present true statements regarding safety, contraindications, side effects, and effectiveness. Brief summary must include all risk information from prescribing information. Reminder advertisements and help-seeking advertisements have specific requirements. False or misleading safety claims constitute misbranding.
keyRequirements: Brief summary of side effects, contraindications, effectiveness
regulations.notes: Applies to prescription drug advertisements including required safety disclosures

regulations.title: Medication Guides
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes requirements for Medication Guides - FDA-approved patient labeling required when FDA determines information is necessary to prevent serious adverse effects, when patient adherence decisions require safety information, or when product has serious risks requiring patient awareness. Must be distributed with each dispensing. Content must be in understandable language addressing specific serious risks identified through pharmacovigilance.
keyRequirements: FDA determination of Medication Guide necessity
Updates based on new safety information
regulations.notes: Applies to drugs requiring patient-directed safety information

regulations.officialIdentifier: FDA Guidance: Postmarketing Studies and Clinical Trials
regulations.title: Guidance for Industry: Postmarketing Studies and Clinical Trials — Implementation of Section 505(o)(3) of the FD&C Act
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides guidance on FDA's implementation of postmarketing study and clinical trial requirements under FDAAA Section 505(o)(3). Explains when FDA may require PMRs for safety issues, distinction between PMRs (required) and PMCs (committed), study design considerations, timelines, reporting requirements, and consequences of non-compliance. Essential for managing post-approval safety commitments.
regulations.notes: Applies to PMR and PMC studies required by FDA under FDAAA authority

regulations.officialIdentifier: FDA Guidance: FAERS Medication Error Reporting
regulations.title: Guidance for Industry: Safety Reporting Requirements for INDs and BA/BE Studies - Medication Errors
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides guidance on reporting medication errors to FAERS, including errors without patient harm (potential errors) and errors resulting in adverse events. Clarifies that medication errors meeting serious criteria should be reported as expedited ICSRs. Addresses root cause categorization, product quality issues vs use errors, labeling/packaging contributions, and error prevention strategies.
keyRequirements: Expedited reporting for serious errors with AEs
regulations.notes: Applies to medication error reporting and case processing in FAERS

regulations.officialIdentifier: FDA Guidance: Suicidality in Drug Evaluation
regulations.title: Guidance for Industry: Suicidal Ideation and Behavior — Prospective Assessment of Occurrence in Clinical Trials
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides recommendations for prospective assessment of suicidal ideation and behavior in clinical trials. Recommends use of Columbia-Suicide Severity Rating Scale (C-SSRS) or similar validated instrument. Applies to drugs for psychiatric indications, epilepsy, and other CNS conditions. Addresses baseline assessment, ongoing monitoring, data analysis, and labeling implications for identified signals.
regulations.notes: Applies to CNS drugs and others with suicidality risk; clinical trial assessment

regulations.title: Guidance for Industry: Drug-Induced Liver Injury — Premarketing Clinical Evaluation
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides guidance on premarketing evaluation of drug-induced liver injury potential. Defines Hy's Law criteria (ALT >3x ULN with bilirubin >2x ULN without cholestasis) as predictor of severe DILI. Addresses liver chemistry monitoring, stopping rules, causality assessment, and signal detection. Critical for identifying hepatotoxic signals before and after approval.
keyRequirements: Liver chemistry monitoring (ALT, AST, ALP, bilirubin)
regulations.notes: Applies to hepatotoxicity assessment; Hy's Law evaluation

regulations.title: Guidance for Industry: E14 Clinical Evaluation of QT/QTc Interval Prolongation and Proarrhythmic Potential for Non-Antiarrhythmic Drugs
regulations.summaryDescription: ICH E14 guidance on clinical evaluation of QT/QTc interval prolongation and proarrhythmic potential. Addresses thorough QT (TQT) study design, concentration-QTc analysis, and integrated risk assessment. Includes FDA Q&A updates on exposure-response analysis as alternative to TQT in some cases. Critical for cardiac safety signal detection during development and post-marketing.
regulations.notes: Applies to non-antiarrhythmic drugs; TQT studies and cardiac safety monitoring

regulations.title: Guidance for Industry: Immunogenicity Assessment for Therapeutic Protein Products
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides guidance on immunogenicity assessment for therapeutic protein products including antibodies, enzymes, cytokines, and fusion proteins. Addresses anti-drug antibody (ADA) assay development, testing strategies, clinical consequences (loss of efficacy, hypersensitivity, cross-reactivity), and risk-based approaches. Critical for biologics safety monitoring throughout product lifecycle.
regulations.notes: Applies to therapeutic proteins; anti-drug antibody monitoring

regulations.title: Guidance for Industry: Clinical Lactation Studies — Study Design, Data Analysis, and Recommendations for Labeling
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides guidance on clinical lactation studies to support labeling under the Pregnancy and Lactation Labeling Rule (PLLR). Addresses study design for measuring drug concentration in breast milk, infant exposure estimation, and clinical relevance assessment. Supports the Lactation subsection of labeling with human data rather than relying solely on animal studies.
keyRequirements: Study design for breast milk sampling
regulations.notes: Applies to PLLR compliance; lactation safety data

regulations.officialIdentifier: FDA Guidance: IND Safety Reporting Aggregate Analysis
regulations.title: Guidance for Industry: Safety Assessment for IND Safety Reporting
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides guidance on when and how to conduct aggregate safety analyses to determine reporting obligations under 21 CFR 312.32. Addresses the distinction between individual case assessment and aggregate/cumulative analysis, signal detection methodology, and determining when an adverse event meets the 'suspected' relationship criterion. Emphasizes evidence-based approach to causality assessment.
regulations.notes: Applies to aggregate safety analysis for IND safety reporting decisions

regulations.title: Framework for FDA's Real-World Evidence Program
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes FDA's framework for evaluating real-world evidence (RWE) derived from real-world data (RWD) to support regulatory decisions. RWD sources include EHRs, claims data, registries, and digital health technologies. Addresses data quality, study design considerations, and regulatory applications. Increasingly relevant for post-market safety surveillance, signal validation, and effectiveness studies in broader populations.
regulations.notes: Applies to use of RWD/RWE for regulatory decisions including safety evaluation

regulations.officialIdentifier: FDA Program: Rare Pediatric Disease Priority Review Voucher
regulations.title: Rare Pediatric Disease Priority Review Voucher Program
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides priority review voucher (PRV) to sponsors of approved rare pediatric disease drug applications. Incentivizes development of drugs for serious, life-threatening rare diseases primarily affecting children. Relevant to pharmacovigilance as it encourages generation of pediatric safety data in underserved populations. Vouchers transferable and valuable, creating market incentive for pediatric drug development.
keyRequirements: NDA/BLA approval for rare pediatric indication
regulations.notes: Applies to drugs for rare pediatric diseases; incentivizes pediatric safety data generation

regulations.title: FDA Cooperative Research and Development Agreements (CRADA) and Research Collaborations
regulations.summaryDescription: Enables formal research collaborations between FDA and external partners through Cooperative Research and Development Agreements (CRADAs) and other mechanisms. Provides potential access to FDA safety databases (FAERS, Sentinel) for qualified researchers. Supports pharmacovigilance method development, signal detection research, and safety science advancement. ORISE fellowship programs place researchers at FDA for safety-related projects.
keyRequirements: Research objectives alignment with FDA priorities
regulations.notes: Applies to research partnerships accessing FDA safety databases for pharmacovigilance research

regulations.title: Pharmacovigilance Directive - Amending Directive 2001/83/EC
regulations.summaryDescription: Fundamental EU pharmacovigilance directive amending 2001/83/EC. Establishes modernized PV framework including: mandatory EudraVigilance reporting, QPPV requirements, PSMF obligations, PSUR submission via repository, signal management responsibilities, RMP requirements, and enhanced transparency. Forms legal basis for GVP modules alongside Regulation 1235/2010. Implemented December 2010, provisions phased in through July 2012.
keyRequirements: RMP for new MAs and when requested
ADR reporting by HCPs and patients
regulations.notes: Core EU pharmacovigilance legal framework for nationally authorized products

regulations.title: Pharmacovigilance Regulation - Amending Regulation (EC) No 726/2004
regulations.summaryDescription: Fundamental EU pharmacovigilance regulation amending 726/2004 for centrally authorized products. Establishes PRAC (Pharmacovigilance Risk Assessment Committee), enhanced EMA role in signal detection, Union referral procedures, and centralized safety assessment. Directly applicable in all Member States without transposition. Works in tandem with Directive 2010/84/EU to form complete EU PV legal framework.
keyRequirements: Public hearings for major safety issues
regulations.notes: Core EU pharmacovigilance legal framework for centrally authorized products

regulations.title: Falsified Medicines Directive - Safety Features (Serialization)
regulations.summaryDescription: Implements Directive 2011/62/EU (Falsified Medicines Directive) safety features. Requires unique identifier (2D barcode) and anti-tampering device on prescription medicine packaging. Enables end-to-end verification through European Medicines Verification System (EMVS). PV relevance: supports product traceability for safety investigations, recall effectiveness, and counterfeit detection which may present as adverse events.
regulations.notes: Applies to prescription medicines requiring safety features (serialization, tamper evidence)

regulations.title: Public Participation in Pharmacovigilance
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes framework for patient and healthcare professional participation in EU pharmacovigilance. Covers patient reporting of suspected ADRs, public access to pharmacovigilance data, patient involvement in regulatory processes, and transparency requirements. Emphasizes value of patient-reported outcomes and real-world patient experience in safety assessment.
keyRequirements: Public hearings for significant safety issues
regulations.notes: Applies to patient and public involvement in pharmacovigilance activities

regulations.title: Continuous Pharmacovigilance - Post-Authorisation Safety Studies
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides guidance on continuous pharmacovigilance activities throughout product lifecycle. Covers ongoing benefit-risk monitoring, signal detection methodologies (spontaneous, literature, registries), data mining techniques, and integration of multiple data sources. Emphasizes proactive safety surveillance using EudraVigilance data, healthcare databases, and real-world evidence.
regulations.notes: Applies to ongoing pharmacovigilance beyond PASS including signal detection methodologies

regulations.title: Pharmacovigilance for Pregnant and Breastfeeding Women
regulations.summaryDescription: Draft guidance on pharmacovigilance approaches specific to pregnant and breastfeeding women. Addresses pregnancy registry design, outcome data collection, exposure timing importance, teratogenicity signal detection, lactation safety assessment, and labeling recommendations. Aligns with FDA PLLR approach while addressing EU-specific requirements including pregnancy prevention programme oversight.
regulations.notes: Applies to pharmacovigilance considerations for pregnancy and lactation

regulations.title: Medical Device Regulation (MDR)
regulations.summaryDescription: EU Medical Device Regulation establishing comprehensive framework for medical devices. Critical for drug-device combination products where both PV and device vigilance apply. Establishes EUDAMED database, Unique Device Identification (UDI), and vigilance reporting for device incidents. MAHs of combination products must coordinate pharmacovigilance with device vigilance requirements.
regulations.notes: Applies to drug-device combinations; establishes device vigilance parallel to pharmacovigilance

regulations.title: In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation (IVDR)
regulations.summaryDescription: EU In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation for diagnostic devices. Critical for companion diagnostics that guide drug therapy (e.g., HER2 testing for trastuzumab, PD-L1 for immunotherapy). Vigilance for companion diagnostics must be coordinated with pharmacovigilance of associated medicinal products. Test failures can result in inappropriate drug exposure.
regulations.notes: Applies to companion diagnostics and IVDs used with medicinal products

regulations.title: HERA Regulation - Serious Cross-Border Health Threats
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes Health Emergency Preparedness and Response Authority (HERA) and reinforces EMA's role during public health emergencies. Enhances pharmacovigilance requirements for medicinal products authorized under emergency procedures. Includes provisions for real-time safety monitoring, expedited signal assessment, and coordinated EU response to safety issues during crises.
keyRequirements: Emergency Task Force (ETF) for safety assessment
regulations.notes: Applies to EMA reinforced role during health emergencies; enhanced PV for emergency-authorized products

regulations.title: Veterinary Medicinal Products Regulation
regulations.summaryDescription: EU regulation on veterinary medicinal products establishing pharmacovigilance for veterinary products. Relevant to human PV through One Health approach: zoonotic disease vaccines, antimicrobial resistance surveillance, and residue monitoring in food chain. EudraVigilance Veterinary (EVVet) parallel system. Important cross-reference for companies with both human and veterinary portfolios.
regulations.notes: Cross-reference for One Health approach; antimicrobial resistance monitoring

regulations.title: Guideline on the Investigation of Drug Interactions
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive guideline on investigating drug-drug, drug-food, and drug-herbal interactions. Addresses in vitro and clinical study design, CYP450 and transporter assessments, and labeling recommendations. PV relevance: drug interaction signals are common post-market findings requiring label updates and safety communications.
regulations.notes: Applies to drug interaction studies with safety implications

regulations.title: ICH E14 Implementation - QT/QTc Studies and Proarrhythmic Risk
regulations.summaryDescription: EMA implementation of ICH E14 on QT/QTc interval prolongation evaluation. Covers thorough QT study design, exposure-response analysis, and integrated cardiac safety assessment. Critical for identifying proarrhythmic risk during development. Post-market relevance: cardiac safety signals require rapid assessment and potential labeling changes or withdrawal.
regulations.notes: Applies to cardiac safety assessment; TQT studies and concentration-QTc analysis

regulations.title: ICH S7B Implementation - Nonclinical Cardiac Safety (hERG)
regulations.summaryDescription: EMA implementation of ICH S7B on nonclinical cardiac safety evaluation. Covers in vitro hERG assay, action potential studies, and in vivo QT assessment in animals. Integrated with clinical E14 studies for comprehensive proarrhythmic risk assessment. Early identification of cardiac liability enables risk management before clinical exposure.
regulations.notes: Applies to nonclinical cardiac safety studies (hERG, action potential, telemetry)

regulations.title: Guideline on the Evaluation of Anticancer Medicinal Products in Man
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive guideline on anticancer drug development addressing efficacy and safety assessment. Covers dose-finding with safety considerations, secondary malignancy monitoring, cardiotoxicity assessment, and long-term follow-up requirements. Critical for RMP development for oncology products with complex safety profiles.
regulations.notes: Applies to oncology drug development including safety assessment considerations

regulations.title: Guideline on Clinical Investigation of Medicinal Products in the Treatment of Depression
regulations.summaryDescription: Guideline on clinical development of antidepressants addressing efficacy and safety. Includes requirements for systematic suicidality assessment using validated instruments (C-SSRS), withdrawal syndrome evaluation, and long-term safety monitoring. Aligns with FDA suicidality guidance. Critical given antidepressant class-wide suicidality warnings.
regulations.notes: Applies to antidepressant development including suicidality assessment

regulations.title: PRAC Mandate, Objectives and Rules of Procedure
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes the Pharmacovigilance Risk Assessment Committee (PRAC) mandate and procedures. PRAC is responsible for all aspects of pharmacovigilance including signal assessment, PSUR evaluation, RMP assessment, referral opinions, and PASS oversight. Understanding PRAC procedures essential for MAH engagement with EU safety regulatory processes.
regulations.notes: Defines PRAC committee operations and decision-making for EU pharmacovigilance

regulations.title: Article 31/20/107i Referral Procedures
regulations.summaryDescription: Procedures for EU referrals triggered by safety concerns. Article 31 (Directive 2001/83): Union interest referrals for safety/efficacy. Article 20 (Regulation 726/2004): Centralized product referrals. Article 107i: Urgent Union procedure for serious safety concerns requiring rapid action. Critical for understanding how significant safety signals lead to EU-wide regulatory action.
keyRequirements: Article 107i: 60-day urgent procedure for serious risks
regulations.notes: Applies to EU-wide safety referrals for urgent and non-urgent safety concerns

regulations.title: EU Rapid Alert System for Quality Defects
regulations.summaryDescription: EU system for rapid notification of quality defects in medicinal products that may affect patient safety. Covers manufacturing defects, contamination, stability failures, and packaging errors. Parallel to FDA Field Alert Reports. Interface between quality assurance and pharmacovigilance when defects lead to adverse events or potential safety risks.
keyRequirements: Link to adverse event reports if defect-related
regulations.notes: Applies to product quality defects with potential patient safety impact

regulations.title: EudraVigilance Data Analysis System (EVDAS) User Guide
regulations.summaryDescription: Technical guide for using EudraVigilance Data Analysis System (EVDAS). EVDAS provides MAHs access to EudraVigilance data for their products, enabling signal detection through electronic Reaction Monitoring Reports (eRMRs), line listings, and statistical analysis tools. Per GVP Module IX, MAHs must use EudraVigilance data as part of signal detection activities.
regulations.notes: Applies to MAH use of EVDAS for signal detection from EudraVigilance data

regulations.title: EudraVigilance Registration and Access Guide
regulations.summaryDescription: Guide for MAH registration and access to EudraVigilance systems including ICSR submission gateway, EVDAS, and XEVMPD. Covers organization registration, user account setup, digital certificate requirements, and system testing. Prerequisite for electronic ICSR submission and signal detection access in EU.
regulations.notes: Applies to MAH registration and access to EudraVigilance systems

regulations.title: CMDh Best Practice Guide on PSUR Worksharing and Safety Variations
regulations.summaryDescription: CMDh guidance on PSUR single assessment (worksharing) for nationally authorized products and coordination of safety variations. Complements PRAC procedures for centralized products. Covers Reference Member State (RMS) selection, worksharing timelines, outcome implementation, and safety variation coordination across Member States.
regulations.notes: Applies to nationally authorized products PSUR worksharing procedures

regulations.title: EMA Regulatory Science Strategy to 2025
regulations.summaryDescription: EMA's strategic vision for regulatory science through 2025 and beyond. Includes pharmacovigilance priorities: big data/AI in signal detection, real-world evidence integration, patient-focused drug development, and digital health integration. Anticipates future PV requirements and provides roadmap for MAH preparation.
regulations.notes: Defines EMA regulatory science priorities including pharmacovigilance modernization

regulations.title: Data Analysis and Real World Interrogation Network
regulations.summaryDescription: EMA-coordinated network providing real-world evidence from European healthcare databases. Enables rapid safety studies, signal validation, and benefit-risk assessment using routine clinical data. Expanding capacity for proactive pharmacovigilance using electronic health records, registries, and claims data across EU Member States.
keyRequirements: Access to federated European healthcare data
Rapid response capability for emerging safety issues
regulations.notes: Applies to real-world data network for regulatory decision-making including safety

regulations.title: Accelerating Clinical Trials in the EU
regulations.summaryDescription: Multi-stakeholder initiative to transform clinical trial environment in Europe. Addresses CTR implementation, decentralized trials, digital technologies, and streamlined safety reporting. Impacts pharmacovigilance through modernized SUSAR reporting, integrated safety databases, and patient-centric trial designs.
regulations.notes: Applies to clinical trial modernization with safety reporting implications

regulations.title: Proposed EU Pharmaceutical Legislation Reform
regulations.summaryDescription: Proposed comprehensive reform of EU pharmaceutical legislation (revising Directive 2001/83/EC and Regulation 726/2004). Includes pharmacovigilance modernization: enhanced transparency, digital PV requirements, environmental risk assessment integration, antimicrobial resistance provisions, and patient access considerations. Expected adoption 2025-2026 with multi-year transition.
regulations.notes: Major reform of EU pharmaceutical legislation with significant PV implications

regulations.title: Guideline on Good Pharmacovigilance Practices for Biological Medicinal Products
regulations.summaryDescription: Guideline addressing pharmacovigilance considerations specific to biological medicinal products. Covers product traceability requirements (batch number, trade name), immunogenicity monitoring and signal detection, biosimilar pharmacovigilance, and post-marketing surveillance for biologics. Complements GVP Module P.II.
regulations.notes: Applies to biologics-specific pharmacovigilance including traceability and immunogenicity

regulations.title: Adverse Drug Reaction Reporting - Prescription Drugs
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes mandatory adverse drug reaction reporting for marketed health products in Canada. MAHs must report serious ADRs within 15 days and serious unexpected ADRs within 15 days. Annual summary reports required. Reports submitted via Canada Vigilance Program. Foundation of Canadian post-market surveillance.
regulations.notes: Core Canadian expedited ADR reporting requirements for prescription drugs

regulations.officialIdentifier: Protecting Canadians from Unsafe Drugs Act (Vanessa's Law)
regulations.title: Vanessa's Law - Enhanced Pharmacovigilance Authority
regulations.summaryDescription: Landmark Canadian legislation (2014) strengthening Health Canada's pharmacovigilance powers. Enables mandatory ADR reporting by healthcare institutions, recall authority, power to compel safety studies, increased penalties for non-compliance, and authority to require label changes. Named after Vanessa Young who died from an ADR.
keyRequirements: Clinical trial registration and results disclosure
regulations.notes: Enhanced Health Canada authority for post-market safety surveillance

regulations.title: Guidance Document: Submission of Periodic Safety Update Reports and Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Reports
regulations.summaryDescription: Health Canada guidance on PSUR/PBRER submission requirements. Accepts ICH E2C(R2) PBRER format. Submission frequency aligned with international birth date. Canadian-specific content may be requested. Reports reviewed as part of ongoing benefit-risk assessment.
regulations.notes: Canadian requirements for periodic safety update reports

regulations.title: Guidance Document: Submission of Risk Management Plans and Follow-up Commitments
regulations.summaryDescription: Health Canada guidance on RMP submission and content. Accepts EU RMP format. Required for new active substances, biosimilars, and when significant safety concerns identified. Canadian-specific risk minimization measures may be required. RMP updates submitted as part of lifecycle management.
regulations.notes: Canadian risk management plan requirements

regulations.title: Canada Vigilance Program - National ADR Collection and Analysis
regulations.summaryDescription: Health Canada's post-market surveillance program collecting and analyzing adverse reaction reports for health products. Accepts reports from MAHs, healthcare professionals, and consumers. MedEffect Canada is public-facing portal. Data used for signal detection and regulatory action. Canadian equivalent of FDA FAERS/EU EudraVigilance.
keyRequirements: MAH report submission via CV Online
Public data access via Canada Vigilance Adverse Reaction Online Database
regulations.notes: Canada's national adverse reaction monitoring program

regulations.title: Special Access Programme - Safety Reporting Requirements
regulations.summaryDescription: Safety reporting requirements for drugs provided through Health Canada's Special Access Programme (emergency access to unauthorized drugs). Manufacturers must report serious ADRs. Physicians must report outcomes. Parallel to FDA Expanded Access and EU Compassionate Use. Critical for tracking safety of unapproved treatments.
keyRequirements: Aggregate safety data for marketed applications
regulations.notes: Safety reporting for drugs accessed via Special Access Programme

regulations.title: Guidance Document: Signal Assessment and Management
regulations.summaryDescription: Health Canada guidance on signal detection, validation, prioritization, and assessment. MAHs responsible for ongoing signal detection from all sources including Canada Vigilance data. Health Canada conducts independent signal detection. Signal assessment may lead to label changes, DHPC, or regulatory action.
keyRequirements: Continuous signal detection from all sources
regulations.notes: Health Canada signal detection and assessment framework

regulations.title: Notice of Compliance with Conditions (NOC/c) - Post-Market Commitments
regulations.summaryDescription: Health Canada's conditional approval pathway for promising therapies with incomplete evidence. Requires post-market commitments including confirmatory studies, enhanced ADR monitoring, and specific pharmacovigilance activities. Marketed with Letter of Undertaking. Similar to FDA Accelerated Approval and EU Conditional MA.
regulations.notes: Canadian conditional approval with enhanced PV requirements

regulations.title: Risk Management Measures and Distribution Restrictions
regulations.summaryDescription: Health Canada framework for risk management measures beyond labeling, similar to FDA REMS and EU additional risk minimization measures. Includes controlled distribution programs, patient registries, healthcare professional training, and patient acknowledgment forms. Required for high-risk products (e.g., thalidomide, isotretinoin, clozapine).
regulations.notes: Canadian controlled distribution and risk minimization programs

regulations.title: Clinical Trial Safety Reporting Requirements - Food and Drug Regulations Part C, Division 5
regulations.summaryDescription: Canadian clinical trial safety reporting under Food and Drug Regulations Division 5. Sponsors must report serious unexpected ADRs within 15 days (7 days if fatal/life-threatening). Annual safety reports required. Clinical Trial Application (CTA) framework. Aligned with ICH E2A and E6(R3).
keyRequirements: DSEN (Drug Safety and Effectiveness Network) collaboration
regulations.notes: Safety reporting requirements for clinical trials conducted in Canada

regulations.officialIdentifier: PMD Act (Pharmaceutical and Medical Device Act)
regulations.title: Act on Securing Quality, Efficacy and Safety of Products Including Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices
regulations.summaryDescription: Japan's primary pharmaceutical law (formerly Pharmaceutical Affairs Law). Establishes PMDA authority, marketing authorization requirements, and post-market safety obligations. Requires ADR reporting, periodic safety reports, post-marketing surveillance studies, and re-examination. Foundation for all Japanese pharmacovigilance activities.
regulations.notes: Core Japanese pharmaceutical legislation establishing PV requirements

regulations.title: Good Vigilance Practice - Japanese GVP Standards
regulations.summaryDescription: Japanese ministerial ordinance establishing Good Vigilance Practice requirements for marketing authorization holders. Covers PV system organization, qualified persons, SOPs, ADR reporting, safety measures, quality management, and record keeping. Basis for PMDA PV inspections. Aligned with ICH principles but with Japan-specific requirements.
regulations.notes: Japanese Good Vigilance Practice standards for MAHs

regulations.title: Good Post-Marketing Study Practice
regulations.summaryDescription: Ministerial ordinance establishing Good Post-Marketing Study Practice for conducting post-marketing surveillance in Japan. Covers Drug Use-Results Surveys, Post-Marketing Clinical Studies, and Special Drug Use-Results Surveys. Mandatory for re-examination period. Protocol design, data collection, analysis, and reporting requirements.
keyRequirements: GCP elements for PM clinical studies
regulations.notes: Japanese standards for post-marketing surveillance studies

regulations.title: Adverse Drug Reaction Reporting Rules and Timelines
regulations.summaryDescription: PMDA requirements for adverse drug reaction reporting. Known serious ADRs reported within 30 days; unknown/unexpected serious ADRs within 15 days. Domestic and foreign reports required. Reports submitted via PMDA Gateway in J-E2B format. Includes infection reports for biologics (within 15 days).
keyRequirements: Measures taken in foreign countries: 15 days
regulations.notes: Japanese expedited and periodic ADR reporting requirements

regulations.title: Japanese E2B(R3) Implementation Guide for ICSR Submissions
regulations.summaryDescription: Japanese implementation guide for E2B(R3) electronic ICSR submissions to PMDA. Defines J-E2B data elements, Japanese-specific extensions, validation rules, and PMDA Gateway technical requirements. Essential for MAH system configuration for Japan reporting.
regulations.notes: Technical specifications for PMDA Gateway ICSR submissions

regulations.title: Post-Approval Re-examination of New Drugs
regulations.summaryDescription: Japan-unique requirement for re-examination of new drugs after marketing. Typically 8 years for new active substances (4-10 years depending on product type). MAH must submit re-examination application with GPSP surveillance data. PMDA reviews efficacy and safety in real-world use. Failure may result in approval revocation.
keyRequirements: Conduct GPSP surveillance during re-examination period
regulations.notes: Unique Japanese system for post-approval re-evaluation

regulations.title: Enhanced Surveillance During Initial Marketing Period
regulations.summaryDescription: Japanese requirement for intensified safety monitoring during first 6 months after launch. Applies to products with limited Japanese clinical experience. MAH must proactively collect safety information, promptly communicate with prescribers, provide MRs with safety information, and rapidly respond to emerging safety signals. Similar concept to EU additional monitoring but Japan-specific implementation.
regulations.notes: Six-month intensive monitoring for newly launched products in Japan

regulations.title: PMDA Safety Communications and Precautions
regulations.summaryDescription: PMDA safety communication framework including Yellow Letter (Kinkyū Anzen-sei Jōhō - Emergency Safety Information), Blue Letter (Anzen-sei Sokuhō - Rapid Safety Communication), Precautions Revision notifications, and PMDA Medical Safety Information. MAHs must respond to PMDA requests and distribute safety information to healthcare providers.
keyRequirements: Yellow Letter for urgent safety issues
regulations.notes: PMDA safety information dissemination system

regulations.title: Periodic Safety Update Report Requirements for Japan
regulations.summaryDescription: PMDA requirements for periodic safety reports. ICH E2C(R2) PBRER format accepted. Submission frequency per approval conditions and re-examination period. Japanese-specific addendum may be required covering Japan market data. Used for ongoing benefit-risk assessment and re-examination preparation.
regulations.notes: Japanese periodic safety report requirements

regulations.title: Guideline on Risk Management Plan for Pharmaceutical Products
regulations.summaryDescription: PMDA guidance on Risk Management Plan submission and content. Japanese RMP format differs from EU RMP but covers similar elements: safety specification, pharmacovigilance plan, and risk minimization activities. Required for new active substances. Published on PMDA website for transparency. Updates throughout product lifecycle.
regulations.notes: Japanese Risk Management Plan requirements

regulations.title: Therapeutic Goods Regulations 1990 - Pharmacovigilance Provisions
regulations.summaryDescription: Australian regulations implementing Therapeutic Goods Act 1989 for pharmacovigilance. Establishes sponsor obligations, ADR reporting timelines, periodic safety report requirements, and TGA enforcement powers. Sponsors must maintain PV systems and report safety information to TGA. Foundation for all Australian PV compliance.
keyRequirements: Serious ADR reporting within 15 calendar days
regulations.notes: Implementing regulations for Australian PV requirements

regulations.title: Reporting Adverse Events - Medicine and Vaccine Sponsors
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive TGA guidance on adverse event reporting for medicine and vaccine sponsors. Covers report types, timelines, submission channels (TBS portal, E2B), case assessment, follow-up requirements, and quality standards. Essential reference for sponsor ICSR submissions to TGA.
regulations.notes: TGA guidance on sponsor adverse event reporting procedures

regulations.title: Periodic Safety Update Reports for Prescription Medicines
regulations.summaryDescription: TGA guidance on PSUR/PBRER submission for prescription medicines. Accepts ICH E2C(R2) PBRER format. Submission frequency aligned with international birth date. Australian-specific data may be requested. Reports inform TGA ongoing benefit-risk assessment and regulatory decisions.
regulations.notes: Australian requirements for periodic safety update reports

regulations.title: Australian Risk Management Plan Requirements
regulations.summaryDescription: TGA guidance on Risk Management Plan requirements for medicines. Accepts EU RMP format with Australian adaptations. Required for new chemical entities, biosimilars, and products with significant safety concerns. Australian-specific risk minimization may be required. RMP updates throughout product lifecycle.
regulations.notes: Australian risk management plan requirements

regulations.title: Australian Register of Therapeutic Goods - Product Identification for PV
regulations.summaryDescription: The Australian Register of Therapeutic Goods (ARTG) is the official database of approved therapeutic goods. ARTG numbers required for accurate ICSR reporting and product identification. Public searchable database. Essential for sponsor PV systems to correctly identify products in adverse event reports.
regulations.notes: Australian product registration database critical for PV reporting

regulations.title: Provisional Approval - Enhanced Pharmacovigilance Requirements
regulations.summaryDescription: TGA pathway for early access to promising medicines with incomplete data. Two-year initial approval with annual renewals. Requires enhanced PV including specific conditions, additional reporting requirements, and confirmatory data submission. Similar to FDA Accelerated Approval and EU Conditional MA.
regulations.notes: TGA provisional registration with enhanced safety monitoring

regulations.title: Australian Black Triangle Scheme for Additional Monitoring
regulations.summaryDescription: TGA's black triangle (▼) scheme identifying medicines subject to additional monitoring. Applies to new active substances, biosimilars, and products with specific safety concerns. Encourages enhanced ADR reporting from healthcare professionals. Symbol displayed in Product Information. Review for removal typically after adequate safety data accumulated.
keyRequirements: Review for removal after adequate data
regulations.notes: Australian enhanced monitoring for newly registered medicines

regulations.title: TGA Signal Detection and Investigation Process
regulations.summaryDescription: TGA process for detecting, validating, and responding to safety signals. Uses DAEN (Database of Adverse Event Notifications) and international data sources. Sponsors expected to conduct ongoing signal detection and communicate significant findings to TGA. TGA publishes safety communications (alerts, updates) based on confirmed signals.
regulations.notes: TGA signal detection and regulatory response framework

regulations.title: Swiss Ordinance on Medicinal Products - Pharmacovigilance Requirements
regulations.summaryDescription: Swiss federal ordinance implementing Therapeutic Products Act (HMG) pharmacovigilance provisions. Establishes authorization holder obligations, ADR reporting timelines (15 days serious, 60 days non-serious), periodic safety reports, signal management, and Swissmedic oversight. Strong alignment with EU GVP but independent Swiss implementation.
regulations.notes: Swiss implementing ordinance for PV under Heilmittelgesetz

regulations.title: Swissmedic Electronic Vigilance System (ElViS) User Guide
regulations.summaryDescription: Technical guide for Swissmedic's Electronic Vigilance System (ElViS) for ICSR submissions. Covers E2B(R3) format requirements, Swiss-specific data elements, system registration, submission procedures, and validation rules. Mandatory for all authorization holder ICSR submissions to Swissmedic.
regulations.notes: Technical specifications for Swiss ICSR submissions

regulations.title: Periodic Safety Update Report Requirements for Switzerland
regulations.summaryDescription: Swissmedic guidance on PSUR/PBRER submission. Accepts ICH E2C(R2) PBRER format. Submission frequency aligned with international birth date. Swiss-specific data may be requested. ICH guidelines automatically applicable in Switzerland at Step 4 adoption. Strong EU alignment.
regulations.notes: Swiss requirements for periodic safety update reports

regulations.title: Risk Management Plan Requirements for Switzerland
regulations.summaryDescription: Swissmedic guidance on RMP submission and content. Accepts EU RMP format. Required for new active substances and products with significant safety concerns. Swiss-specific risk minimization measures may be required. Strong EU alignment facilitates parallel EU/Swiss submissions.
regulations.notes: Swiss risk management plan requirements

regulations.title: Clinical Trial Safety Reporting Requirements Post-Brexit
regulations.summaryDescription: UK clinical trial safety reporting requirements under national CT regulations post-Brexit. Sponsors must report SUSARs within 7 days (fatal/life-threatening) or 15 days (other serious). Annual safety reports required. MHRA notification of urgent safety measures. Separate from but aligned with EU CTR where NI products involved.
regulations.notes: UK clinical trial safety reporting under Medicines for Human Use (Clinical Trials) Regulations

regulations.title: MHRA Defect Reporting Centre - Quality Defects and Recalls
regulations.summaryDescription: MHRA's Defect Reporting Centre handles quality defect notifications that may impact patient safety. UK equivalent to FDA Field Alert Reports and EU Rapid Alert System. Covers contamination, labeling errors, packaging defects, and stability failures. Interface between quality assurance and pharmacovigilance when defects lead to potential or actual patient harm.
keyRequirements: Interface with Yellow Card for related ADRs
regulations.notes: UK system for reporting product quality defects with safety implications

regulations.title: UK Framework for Paediatric Medicines Regulation Post-Brexit
regulations.summaryDescription: Post-Brexit UK framework for paediatric medicines regulation. MHRA maintains pediatric requirements similar to EU Paediatric Regulation but with UK-specific implementation. Paediatric Investigation Plan (PIP) equivalent requirements. Enhanced pediatric safety monitoring and labeling. MHRA guidance on pediatric extrapolation and safety assessment.
regulations.notes: UK requirements for paediatric medicines development including safety assessment

regulations.title: Medication Errors
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. MEDICATION ERROR DEFINITION:
   - Any unintentional error in prescribing, dispensing, administering, or monitoring
   - May or may not result in patient harm
   - Includes potential errors (intercepted before reaching patient)

2. REPORTING REQUIREMENTS:
   - Medication errors with adverse reactions: Report as ICSR to EudraVigilance
   - Medication errors without harm: Report per GVP requirements
   - Associated product quality issues: Coordinate with quality systems

3. ICSR CONSIDERATIONS:
   - Use medication error MedDRA terms
   - Document error type and circumstances
   - Include contributing factors
   - Report outcome (including no harm)

4. SIGNAL DETECTION:
   - Monitor for patterns of medication errors
   - Assess product-related factors (name, packaging, labeling)
   - Evaluate human factors

5. RISK MINIMIZATION:
   - Labeling improvements
   - Packaging changes
   - Educational materials
   - Name confusion prevention
keyRequirements: Risk minimization measures for error prevention
regulations.notes: EU - GVP Module XVII on medication errors. Published March 2024. Addresses collection, management, reporting of medication errors with/without harm. Critical for ICSR classification.

regulations.title: EU Medicines Agencies Network Strategy to 2028
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. STRATEGIC PRIORITIES:
   - Enabling data-driven regulation
   - Tackling health crises and ensuring supply
   - Integrating innovation into regulation
   - Enhancing agility and efficiency
   - Strengthening the European network
   - Supporting sustainability

2. PV-RELEVANT ELEMENTS:
   - Real-world evidence integration
   - AI/ML in regulatory processes
   - Signal detection enhancement
   - DARWIN EU expansion
   - EudraVigilance modernization

3. DATA STRATEGY:
   - European Health Data Space alignment
   - Interoperability standards
   - Big data analytics capability

4. TIMELINE:
   - 2024-2028 implementation
   - Annual progress reporting
   - Mid-term review 2026
keyRequirements: Strategic direction for EU PV modernization
regulations.notes: EU - Joint EMA-HMA Network Strategy to 2028. Replaces RSS 2025. Six strategic priorities including data-driven regulation, supply chain security, and innovation support. PV modernization embedded.

regulations.title: Data Analysis and Real World Interrogation Network - Expansion Phase
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. EXPANSION SCOPE:
   - Additional European data partners
   - Broader geographic coverage
   - Enhanced therapeutic area coverage
   - Expanded patient populations

2. CAPABILITIES:
   - Rapid safety studies
   - Signal validation
   - Drug utilization studies
   - Comparative effectiveness
   - PASS support

3. DATA SOURCES:
   - Electronic health records
   - Claims databases
   - Disease registries
   - Linked data networks

4. MAH RELEVANCE:
   - May be used for regulatory queries
   - Signal validation reference
   - PASS design consultation
   - Supports benefit-risk assessment

5. TIMELINE:
   - Phase 2: 2024-2026
   - Sustained operation thereafter
keyRequirements: Enhanced RWE capacity for signal validation
regulations.notes: EU - DARWIN EU Phase 2 (2024-2026). Expansion of real-world evidence capacity. More data partners, enhanced analytics, broader geographic coverage. Critical for signal validation and PASS support.

regulations.title: European Health Data Space Regulation
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. PURPOSE:
   - Enable secondary use of health data
   - Support research and innovation
   - Enhance pharmacovigilance capabilities
   - Facilitate cross-border data access

2. PRIMARY USE:
   - Patient access to health data
   - Cross-border healthcare
   - Electronic health records interoperability

3. SECONDARY USE (PV RELEVANT):
   - Research and development
   - Pharmacovigilance and drug safety
   - Regulatory activities
   - Health technology assessment
   - Public health surveillance

4. DATA ACCESS MECHANISMS:
   - Health Data Access Bodies (national)
   - Secure processing environments
   - Standardized data formats
   - GDPR-compliant frameworks

5. PV IMPLICATIONS:
   - Enhanced access to RWE for signal validation
   - PASS study data sources
   - Comparative safety analyses
   - Long-term outcome tracking

6. IMPLEMENTATION:
   - Phased 2025-2027
   - Member State transposition required
   - Technical infrastructure development
keyRequirements: Secondary use of health data for PV
Cross-border data access for safety studies
regulations.notes: EU - European Health Data Space Regulation. Adopted late 2024. Enables secondary use of health data for research and pharmacovigilance. Major impact on RWE/pharmacoepidemiology access.

regulations.title: Collection, Management and Submission of Reports of Suspected Adverse Reactions to Medicinal Products (Revision 3)
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. KEY UPDATES FROM REV 2:
   - Enhanced ICSR quality requirements
   - Updated medication error guidance (links to Module XVII)
   - Clarified special situations (pregnancy, abuse, misuse)
   - Updated EudraVigilance submission requirements
   - Enhanced duplicate detection guidance

2. ICSR MINIMUM CRITERIA:
   - Identifiable reporter
   - Identifiable patient
   - Suspect medicinal product
   - Suspected adverse reaction

3. REPORTING TIMELINES:
   - 15 days: Serious from EEA
   - 90 days: Non-serious from EEA
   - 15 days: Serious from third countries (if unexpected)

4. SPECIAL SITUATIONS:
   - Pregnancy and lactation exposure
   - Medication errors (with/without harm)
   - Abuse, misuse, overdose, off-label use
   - Lack of efficacy
   - Occupational exposure
   - Transmitted infectious agents

5. QUALITY REQUIREMENTS:
   - MedDRA coding accuracy
   - Narrative completeness
   - Follow-up diligence
   - Duplicate management
regulations.notes: EU - GVP Module VI Revision 3. Major 2024 update. Enhanced guidance on ICSR quality, special situations, medication errors, and EudraVigilance submission requirements. Supersedes Rev 2.

regulations.title: Joint Big Data Steering Group - AI and Data Analytics in Medicines Regulation
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. SCOPE:
   - AI/ML in regulatory decision-making
   - Real-world evidence strategy
   - Data analytics standardization
   - Network capability building

2. KEY WORKSTREAMS:
   - DARWIN EU oversight
   - AI workplan implementation
   - Data quality standards
   - Methodology development

3. DELIVERABLES:
   - HMA-EMA Joint Big Data Task Force reports
   - AI reflection papers
   - Data quality frameworks
   - Pilot study coordination

4. PV RELEVANCE:
   - Signal detection methodology
   - AI-assisted case processing guidance
   - RWE for benefit-risk assessment
   - EudraVigilance analytics enhancement

5. MAH ENGAGEMENT:
   - Public consultations
   - Stakeholder workshops
   - Industry collaboration opportunities
regulations.notes: EU - HMA-EMA Joint Big Data Steering Group. Coordinates AI/data analytics strategy across EU regulatory network. Oversees DARWIN EU, RWE initiatives, and AI implementation in PV.

regulations.title: EU Pharmaceutical Legislation Reform - Commission Proposals
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. PROPOSAL OVERVIEW:
   - Replace Directive 2001/83/EC with new Directive
   - Revise Regulation (EC) 726/2004
   - Modernize EU pharmaceutical framework
   - Address innovation, access, and sustainability

2. PROPOSED PV CHANGES:
   - Enhanced EudraVigilance role
   - Streamlined PSUR procedures
   - Simplified RMP requirements for generics
   - Environmental risk assessment integration
   - Antimicrobial resistance provisions

3. REGULATORY DATA PROTECTION:
   - Changes to data exclusivity periods
   - Conditional incentives
   - Impact on PV timelines

4. ATMP PROVISIONS:
   - Separate ATMP regulation planned
   - Enhanced Hospital Exemption framework
   - CGT-specific requirements

5. TIMELINE:
   - Commission proposal: April 2023
   - Parliament/Council review: 2024-2025
   - Expected adoption: 2025/2026
   - Implementation: Multi-year transition

6. MAH PREPARATION:
   - Monitor legislative progress
   - Assess operational impact
   - Plan system updates
regulations.notes: EU - Major pharmaceutical legislation reform. April 2023 proposals to replace Directive 2001/83/EC and revise Regulation 726/2004. Significant PV system changes anticipated. Multi-year legislative process.

regulations.title: Format for the Periodic Safety Update Report in the EU
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
   - ICH E2C(R2) PBRER format as basis
   - EU-specific elements
   - PSUR Repository submission specifications
   - Document structure requirements

2. EU-SPECIFIC SECTIONS:
   - EU Reference Date compliance
   - EEA exposure data
   - EU signal assessment
   - PRAC recommendation compliance
   - EU-specific actions

3. SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS:
   - PSUR Repository (mandatory)
   - Single assessment procedure documents
   - Supporting documents
   - Cover letter requirements

4. ASSESSMENT INTERFACE:
   - PRAC assessment timeline
   - Lead Member State procedures
   - Worksharing for generics
   - Outcome communication

5. QUALITY STANDARDS:
   - Completeness requirements
   - Data presentation standards
   - Signal evaluation expectations
regulations.notes: EU - GVP Annex IV specifying PSUR format for EU submission via PSUR Repository. Complements Module VII. Details EU-specific requirements overlaying ICH E2C(R2) PBRER format.

regulations.title: EMA Policy on Publication of Clinical Data for Medicinal Products for Human Use
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. SCOPE:
   - Clinical study reports from MA applications
   - Supporting clinical data
   - Post-authorization data submissions

2. PUBLICATION PRINCIPLES:
   - Proactive publication after regulatory decision
   - Anonymization/redaction standards
   - Commercial confidential information protection
   - Personal data protection per GDPR

3. SAFETY DATA COVERAGE:
   - Integrated safety summaries
   - Individual study safety data
   - Adverse event tabulations
   - Subject narratives (anonymized)

4. PV RELEVANCE:
   - Safety data publicly accessible
   - Literature monitoring implications
   - Signal detection by third parties
   - Academic research facilitation

5. MAH CONSIDERATIONS:
   - Redaction proposal preparation
   - Personal data identification
   - CCI justification
   - Timeline planning
regulations.notes: EU - EMA Policy 0070 on clinical data publication. Proactive publication of clinical data from marketing authorization applications. Safety data transparency implications for PV.

regulations.title: PRAC Signal Assessment and Recommendation Procedures
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. EMA SIGNAL DETECTION:
   - EudraVigilance data mining
   - Statistical disproportionality analysis
   - Medical assessment
   - Literature monitoring

2. SIGNAL PRIORITIZATION:
   - Public health impact
   - Strength of evidence
   - Potential for risk minimization
   - Novelty of safety concern

3. MAH INVOLVEMENT:
   - Request for information
   - Response timelines
   - Data provision requirements
   - Assessment review opportunity

4. PRAC ASSESSMENT:
   - Scientific evaluation
   - Benefit-risk reassessment
   - Recommendation formulation
   - CHMP/CMDh referral if needed

5. OUTCOMES:
   - No action required
   - Labeling update
   - Additional studies required
   - Referral procedure initiation
   - Suspension/withdrawal recommendation

6. PUBLICATION:
   - PRAC recommendations published monthly
   - Signal outcomes on EMA website
regulations.notes: EU - PRAC signal assessment procedures. Details EMA signal detection from EudraVigilance, PRAC prioritization, MAH response requirements, and recommendation outcomes.

regulations.title: Signal Management (Revision 2)
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. ANTICIPATED CHANGES (per EU IR 2025/1466):
   - Signal pilot programme termination reflected
   - MAH EudraVigilance monitoring mandatory
   - No standalone signal notification form
   - Signals handled per MAH internal process per IX.B

2. MAH OBLIGATIONS:
   - Continuous safety data monitoring
   - EudraVigilance as additional data source
   - Internal signal detection processes
   - Emerging safety issue notification

3. SIGNAL MANAGEMENT PROCESS:
   - Detection from all sources
   - Validation of signals
   - Prioritization criteria
   - Evaluation methodology
   - Action determination

4. REGULATORY INTERACTION:
   - Communication with NCAs/EMA
   - Response to regulatory requests
   - Referral procedure interface

5. TIMELINE:
   - EU IR 2025/1466 full application: 12 Feb 2026
   - GVP IX Rev 2 expected to align
regulations.notes: EU - GVP Module IX Revision 2 expected following EU IR 2025/1466. Will reflect signal pilot termination, mandatory EV monitoring by MAHs, and updated signal notification procedures.

regulations.title: Veterinary Medicinal Products Regulation
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. VETERINARY PV SYSTEM:
   - EudraVigilance Veterinary (EVVet)
   - Adverse event reporting
   - Signal detection
   - Periodic safety update reports

2. HUMAN PV INTERFACE:
   - Zoonotic products
   - Human exposure to veterinary products
   - Shared manufacturing facilities
   - One Health considerations

3. REPORTING REQUIREMENTS:
   - ADR reporting to EVVet
   - Lack of expected efficacy
   - Environmental incidents
   - Human reactions to veterinary products

4. MAH OBLIGATIONS:
   - Veterinary QPPV
   - PV system maintenance
   - Signal management
   - PSUR submission

5. RELEVANCE:
   - Vaccine manufacturers with both human/vet products
   - Blood product interface
   - Occupational exposure to veterinary products
keyRequirements: Human exposure to vet products reporting
regulations.notes: EU - Veterinary medicinal products regulation. Relevant for cross-over products (vaccines, blood products). Establishes veterinary PV system including EudraVigilance Veterinary (EVVet). Separate from human PV but interface exists.

regulations.title: EudraVigilance Data Analysis System
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. PURPOSE:
   - MAH access to EudraVigilance data
   - Signal detection support
   - Aggregate data analysis
   - Compliance with GVP IX

2. ACCESS REQUIREMENTS:
   - EudraVigilance organization registration
   - Designated EVDAS users
   - Training completion
   - Terms of use acceptance

3. CAPABILITIES:
   - Electronic Reaction Monitoring Reports (eRMRs)
   - Line listings for own products
   - Statistical disproportionality tools
   - Ad hoc queries
   - Data download

4. DATA SCOPE:
   - All EEA ICSRs for own products
   - Aggregate data for reference products
   - Updated regularly
   - Subject to access policy restrictions

5. MAH OBLIGATIONS (per EU IR 2025/1466):
   - Mandatory monitoring of EV as additional data source
   - Integration with internal signal detection
   - Documentation of EVDAS use in signal process

6. TRAINING:
   - EVDAS training modules available
   - Regular updates on functionality
keyRequirements: Mandatory EV monitoring per EU IR 2025/1466
regulations.notes: EU - EudraVigilance Data Analysis System. MAH access tool for signal detection using EudraVigilance data. Provides electronic Reaction Monitoring Reports (eRMRs), line listings, and statistical analysis capabilities.

regulations.title: Extended EudraVigilance Medicinal Product Dictionary
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. PURPOSE:
   - Product identification for ICSRs
   - Regulatory data collection (Article 57)
   - EudraVigilance product matching
   - IDMP transition preparation

2. DATA REQUIREMENTS:
   - Product name (full, short)
   - Active substances
   - Pharmaceutical form
   - Route of administration
   - Strength
   - Marketing authorisation details
   - Package information

3. SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS:
   - Initial submission before first ICSR
   - Updates within 15 days of changes
   - Annual confirmation
   - EV code assignment

4. ICSR LINKAGE:
   - EV codes used in ICSRs
   - Product matching for signal detection
   - Duplicate detection support

5. IDMP TRANSITION:
   - SPOR integration ongoing
   - ISO IDMP standards adoption
   - Phased implementation
   - XEVMPD to PMS migration
regulations.notes: EU - XEVMPD/Article 57 database. Mandatory product registration for EudraVigilance. Required for ICSR submission. Transitioning to IDMP standards via SPOR.

regulations.title: Scientific Advice and Protocol Assistance - Pharmacovigilance Aspects
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. SCOPE:
   - Risk Management Plan development
   - PASS study design
   - Signal management approaches
   - Risk minimization measure effectiveness
   - Benefit-risk methodology
   - PV system design

2. ELIGIBILITY:
   - Pre-authorization sponsors
   - MAHs (post-authorization)
   - Orphan designation holders
   - PRIME designation holders

3. PROCESS:
   - Letter of intent
   - Briefing document submission
   - Written or oral advice
   - SAWP/PRAC evaluation
   - Advice letter issuance

4. TYPES:
   - Standard scientific advice
   - Protocol assistance (orphan)
   - Qualification advice (methods/biomarkers)
   - CHMP/PRAC joint advice

5. PV-SPECIFIC CONSIDERATIONS:
   - RMP structure and content
   - Safety specification development
   - Pharmacovigilance plan design
   - Risk minimization measures
   - PASS protocol development
   - Signal detection methodology
regulations.notes: EU - EMA Scientific Advice for PV-related questions. Covers RMP development, PASS design, signal management approaches, and risk minimization strategies. Available pre- and post-authorization.

regulations.title: SmPC Guideline - Presentation of Safety Information
regulations.summaryDescription:
1. SmPC SAFETY SECTIONS:
   - 4.3 Contraindications
   - 4.4 Special warnings and precautions for use
   - 4.5 Interaction with other medicinal products
   - 4.6 Fertility, pregnancy and lactation
   - 4.7 Effects on ability to drive and use machines
   - 4.8 Undesirable effects
   - 4.9 Overdose

2. SECTION 4.8 REQUIREMENTS:
   - Tabular presentation by SOC
   - Frequency categories (very common to very rare)
   - Class effects identification
   - Post-marketing data inclusion
   - Serious adverse reactions highlight

3. SIGNAL-DRIVEN UPDATES:
   - Variation type determination
   - Urgent safety restrictions
   - Safety labeling change procedure
   - Cross-reference to RMP

4. QRD TEMPLATE:
   - Mandatory template use
   - Standardized terminology
   - Formatting requirements
   - Regular updates

5. HARMONIZATION:
   - EU single SmPC for CAPs
   - CMDh coordination for NAPs
   - PRAC recommendations implementation
regulations.notes: EU - SmPC guideline on safety information presentation. Details Section 4.3-4.9 content requirements. Critical for safety labeling changes and signal-driven updates. QRD templates compliance.

regulations.title: The Extent of Population Exposure to Assess Clinical Safety for Drugs Intended for Long-Term Treatment of Non-Life-Threatening Conditions
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines minimum safety database requirements for NDA/MAA approval. KEY REQUIREMENTS: (1) 1,500 patients exposed to drug, (2) 300-600 patients for 6 months, (3) 100 patients for 12 months for chronic use drugs. Establishes that safety database must be adequate to characterize common adverse events. Foundation for all pre-approval safety assessments. Referenced by FDA, EMA, PMDA for determining adequacy of safety data.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Foundation guideline defining minimum safety database size requirements for marketing authorization.

regulations.title: Safety Pharmacology Studies for Human Pharmaceuticals
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines requirements for safety pharmacology studies assessing potential undesirable pharmacodynamic effects on physiological functions. CORE BATTERY: (1) CNS - motor activity, behavioral changes, coordination, sensory/motor reflexes, body temperature; (2) Cardiovascular - blood pressure, heart rate, ECG; (3) Respiratory - respiratory rate, tidal volume, hemoglobin oxygen saturation. FOLLOW-UP STUDIES: Required when core battery suggests concern. SUPPLEMENTAL STUDIES: Renal, autonomic, GI systems as warranted. Critical for identifying safety signals before human exposure.
keyRequirements: Core battery studies (CNS, CV, respiratory) before first human exposure
Timing relative to clinical development phase
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Core safety pharmacology requirements for CNS, cardiovascular, and respiratory systems.

regulations.title: Nonclinical Evaluation of the Potential for Delayed Ventricular Repolarization (QT Interval Prolongation) by Human Pharmaceuticals
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines nonclinical strategy for assessing potential of drugs to delay ventricular repolarization (QT prolongation). KEY STUDIES: (1) In vitro IKr (hERG) assay - ionic current assessment; (2) In vivo QT study - action potential duration in animals. INTEGRATED RISK ASSESSMENT: Combined with E14 clinical data for comprehensive cardiac safety evaluation. TIMING: Generally before large-scale clinical trials. E14/S7B Q&A (2022) provides updated guidance on integrated nonclinical-clinical assessment allowing flexibility in study requirements.
keyRequirements: In vivo QT study in appropriate species
Integrated assessment with clinical E14 data
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Nonclinical cardiac safety evaluation including hERG assay and in vivo QT studies.

regulations.title: Clinical and Nonclinical Evaluation of QT/QTc Interval Prolongation and Proarrhythmic Potential - Questions and Answers
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive Q&A updating E14 and S7B for modern integrated cardiac safety assessment. KEY UPDATES: (1) Concentration-QTc (C-QTc) analysis from Phase 1 studies can replace dedicated TQT study if negative; (2) Integrated nonclinical-clinical risk assessment; (3) Flexibility in study design based on drug characteristics; (4) Double-negative scenario (negative hERG + negative C-QTc) may eliminate need for TQT; (5) Addresses borderline/positive scenarios requiring additional evaluation. Represents major modernization of cardiac safety paradigm.
keyRequirements: Comprehensive in vitro proarrhythmic assay (CiPA) considerations
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Modernized integrated approach to cardiac safety assessment. Allows concentration-QTc analysis as alternative to thorough QT study.

regulations.title: Ethnic Factors in the Acceptability of Foreign Clinical Data
regulations.summaryDescription: Provides framework for evaluating impact of ethnic factors on drug safety and efficacy for regulatory acceptance of foreign clinical data. INTRINSIC FACTORS: Genetic polymorphisms, age, gender, height/weight, organ dysfunction, disease characteristics. EXTRINSIC FACTORS: Medical practice, diet, smoking, alcohol, pollution, socio-cultural aspects. BRIDGING STUDIES: May be required when drug is ethnically sensitive. KEY FOR PV: Different safety profiles across ethnicities; Signal detection must consider ethnic factors; Regional labeling differences based on ethnic sensitivity.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Framework for assessing ethnic factors affecting drug safety and efficacy across regions.

regulations.title: Studies in Support of Special Populations: Geriatrics
regulations.summaryDescription: Establishes requirements for including geriatric patients in clinical development and assessing drug effects in elderly. KEY REQUIREMENTS: (1) Include patients ≥65 years proportional to disease prevalence; (2) Sufficient numbers ≥75 years for meaningful analysis; (3) PK studies in elderly; (4) Assess age-related safety differences. PV RELEVANCE: Elderly often have different safety profiles; Polypharmacy interactions; Renal/hepatic impairment effects; Falls, cognitive effects, anticholinergic burden. Post-market geriatric-specific signals common.
keyRequirements: Geriatric patients in Phase 3 proportional to target population
Drug-drug interaction assessment relevant to elderly comedications
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Requirements for geriatric patient inclusion in clinical trials and safety evaluation.

regulations.title: Clinical Investigation of Medicinal Products in the Pediatric Population
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive guidance on pediatric drug development. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) Pediatric Study Plan (PSP) - timing and type of studies; (2) Age categories (preterm to adolescent); (3) Extrapolation framework - when adult efficacy can extend to children; (4) Formulation requirements - age-appropriate dosage forms; (5) Long-term safety - growth, development, behavioral endpoints. R1 UPDATE: Enhanced extrapolation guidance, modeling and simulation approaches, ethical considerations. PV RELEVANCE: Pediatric-specific ADRs; Developmental effects; Off-label use monitoring; Growth and maturation impacts.
keyRequirements: Long-term safety studies for developmental effects
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Pediatric clinical development including extrapolation, formulation, and long-term safety.

regulations.title: Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive guidance on statistical principles for clinical trial design and analysis. SAFETY-RELEVANT SECTIONS: (1) Safety/tolerability as secondary endpoint considerations; (2) Multiplicity in safety analyses; (3) Interim analyses for safety; (4) Safety subset analyses; (5) Missing data handling for safety. Foundation document for E9(R1) estimands addendum. KEY CONCEPTS: ITT vs per-protocol populations; Sample size for safety; Subgroup analyses; Data monitoring committees for safety.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Foundation statistical guideline for clinical trials. Basis for E9(R1) estimands addendum.

regulations.title: Choice of Control Group and Related Issues in Clinical Trials
regulations.summaryDescription: Guidance on selecting appropriate control groups for clinical trials. CONTROL TYPES: (1) Placebo; (2) Active (positive) control; (3) Dose-response; (4) External historical; (5) No treatment. SAFETY CONSIDERATIONS: Placebo use ethical constraints when effective treatment exists; Active comparator safety profile considerations; Historical control validity for rare diseases. KEY FOR PV: Comparator safety data generation; Non-inferiority margin safety implications; Background event rates.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Guidance on control group selection including safety considerations for placebo use.

regulations.title: Genomic Sampling and Management of Genomic Data
regulations.summaryDescription: Guidance on incorporating pharmacogenomics into clinical development. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) Prospective genomic sampling strategy; (2) Informed consent for genomic research; (3) Sample collection, storage, handling; (4) Genomic data management and privacy; (5) Regulatory submission of genomic data. PV RELEVANCE: Pharmacogenomic safety markers; Genetic susceptibility to ADRs; Precision medicine dosing; Post-market genomic signal investigation; Companion diagnostic development for safety.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Framework for genomic sample collection and data management in clinical trials.

regulations.title: Bioanalytical Method Validation and Study Sample Analysis
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive guidance on validation of bioanalytical methods used in drug development. SCOPE: Chromatographic methods (LC-MS, GC-MS), ligand binding assays for biologics. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) Full validation parameters - accuracy, precision, selectivity, sensitivity, stability, dilution integrity; (2) Incurred sample reanalysis (ISR); (3) Reference standards and critical reagents; (4) Sample handling and storage. PV RELEVANCE: Supports PK/PD safety assessments; Immunogenicity testing for biologics; Biomarker quantification for safety monitoring; Drug interaction studies; Enables accurate exposure-response analysis critical for safety evaluation.
keyRequirements: Method validation parameters (accuracy, precision, selectivity, sensitivity, range, stability)
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Harmonized guidance on bioanalytical method validation for PK, PD, biomarker, and immunogenicity studies supporting drug development.

regulations.title: Drug Interaction Studies
regulations.summaryDescription: First globally harmonized ICH guideline on drug-drug interaction (DDI) studies. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) In vitro enzyme and transporter studies - CYP450, UGT, transporters (P-gp, BCRP, OATP, OAT, OCT); (2) Clinical DDI studies - study design, probe substrates, DDI prediction; (3) Physiologically-based pharmacokinetic (PBPK) modeling; (4) Labeling recommendations.
