PV RELEVANCE: Critical for safety in polypharmacy; Post-market DDI signals common; Informs concomitant medication warnings; Essential for geriatric safety; Guides clinical hold decisions for serious DDI risk.
keyRequirements: Clinical DDI studies with probe substrates
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Harmonized guidance on design, conduct, and interpretation of drug interaction studies for drug development.

regulations.title: Pharmaceutical Quality System
regulations.summaryDescription: Describes pharmaceutical quality system (PQS) model for lifecycle of medicinal products. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) Management responsibility - quality policy, planning, resource management, communication, review; (2) Continual improvement - product/process monitoring, CAPA, change management, knowledge management; (3) ICH Q8-Q11 integration. QUALITY-SAFETY INTERFACE: Quality system ensures product quality throughout lifecycle; Deviation management may reveal safety signals; OOS/OOT investigations may trigger safety assessments; Change control prevents quality-related safety issues; Complaints handling links to pharmacovigilance.
keyRequirements: Process performance and product quality monitoring
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Describes model for effective pharmaceutical quality system based on ISO quality concepts and applicable GMP regulations.

regulations.title: Comparability of Biotechnological/Biological Products Subject to Changes in Their Manufacturing Process
regulations.summaryDescription: Guidance on demonstrating comparability of biotechnological/biological products following manufacturing process changes. KEY PRINCIPLES: (1) Stepwise approach - analytical → nonclinical → clinical depending on change impact; (2) Product knowledge determines testing requirements; (3) Changes during development vs commercial phase considerations. COMPARABILITY LEVELS: Analytical (quality attributes), Functional/PK (biological activity), Clinical (efficacy/safety). PV RELEVANCE: Manufacturing changes may affect safety profile; Immunogenicity changes post-manufacturing critical; Post-change safety monitoring may be required; Comparability supports biosimilar safety assessments; Links quality to clinical safety.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Principles for demonstrating comparability of biotechnological products before and after manufacturing changes.

regulations.title: Assessment and Control of DNA Reactive (Mutagenic) Impurities in Pharmaceuticals to Limit Potential Carcinogenic Risk
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive framework for identifying, assessing, and controlling mutagenic impurities. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) Hazard assessment using (Q)SAR and Ames test; (2) Threshold of Toxicological Concern (TTC) - 1.5 μg/day for lifetime exposure; (3) Higher limits for shorter durations (LTL concept); (4) Class 1-5 impurity classification; (5) Control strategies. GENOTOXICITY CLASSES: Class 1 - Known mutagenic carcinogens; Class 2 - Known mutagens, unknown carcinogens; Class 3 - Alerting structure, unrelated to drug substance; Class 4 - Alerting structure, same as drug substance; Class 5 - No structural alerts. PV RELEVANCE: Nitrosamine impurities crisis; Post-market recalls for mutagenic impurities; Genotoxicity signal investigation; Risk communication requirements.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Framework for assessing and controlling mutagenic impurities in pharmaceuticals to limit carcinogenic risk.

regulations.title: Preclinical Safety Evaluation of Biotechnology-Derived Pharmaceuticals
regulations.summaryDescription: Guidance on designing nonclinical safety programs for biotechnology-derived pharmaceuticals. KEY PRINCIPLES: (1) Species selection based on pharmacological relevance; (2) Use of relevant animal models; (3) Immunogenicity considerations; (4) Reproductive toxicity study design. STUDY TYPES: Single/repeat dose toxicity, safety pharmacology, reproductive toxicity, genotoxicity (generally not applicable), carcinogenicity (case-by-case). R1 ADDENDUM: Clarified tissue cross-reactivity, enhanced antibody studies, recovery periods. PV RELEVANCE: Nonclinical signals inform clinical safety monitoring; Immunogenicity risk assessment; Target-mediated toxicity prediction; Post-market signal interpretation using nonclinical context.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Guidance on nonclinical safety evaluation of biotechnology-derived pharmaceuticals including recombinant proteins and monoclonal antibodies.

regulations.title: Nonclinical Evaluation for Anticancer Pharmaceuticals
regulations.summaryDescription: Specialized guidance for nonclinical safety evaluation of anticancer drugs recognizing different benefit-risk context. KEY PRINCIPLES: (1) Abbreviated programs acceptable for advanced cancer; (2) Genotoxicity - standard battery not always needed; (3) Carcinogenicity - generally not required; (4) Reproductive toxicity - EFD may be deferred. STUDY TIMING: Supports rapid development for serious disease; Initial Phase 1 needs abbreviated package; Expanded studies before broader population exposure. Q&A ADDENDUM: Clarifies biopharmaceuticals, combination therapy, pediatric considerations, juvenile animal studies. PV RELEVANCE: Toxicity expected with anticancer agents; Benefit-risk assessment critical; Long-term safety monitoring essential; Secondary malignancy surveillance.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Guidance on nonclinical safety evaluation specific to anticancer pharmaceuticals, considering risk-benefit in life-threatening conditions.

regulations.title: International Reporting of Periodic Drug-Safety Update Summaries
regulations.summaryDescription: Established framework for periodic safety update summaries for marketed drugs. KEY CONTRIBUTIONS: (1) Defined PSUR concept and structure; (2) International Birth Date (IBD) concept; (3) Data lock point standardization; (4) Interval vs cumulative analysis approach; (5) Line listing and summary tabulation formats. HISTORICAL SIGNIFICANCE: Direct foundation for ICH E2C (PSUR) and subsequently E2C(R2) (PBRER). Introduced concepts still used today including world-wide marketing authorization status, exposure estimation, and benefit-risk framework for periodic reports. While superseded by ICH E2C(R2) for format, core concepts remain foundational.
regulations.notes: International - Foundation document for periodic safety update reports. Basis for ICH E2C development.

regulations.title: Guidelines for Preparing Core Clinical-Safety Information on Drugs
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines approach for preparing Company Core Safety Information (CCSI) and Core Data Sheet (CDS). KEY CONCEPTS: (1) CCSI as reference document for global safety information; (2) CDS as basis for local labeling; (3) Standardized safety sections; (4) Adverse reaction terminology and frequency conventions; (5) Special populations safety information. ONGOING RELEVANCE: CCSI/CDS concept remains industry standard for managing global product safety information. Referenced in ICH E2C(R2) for determining expectedness. Foundation for reference safety information (RSI) used in clinical development. Critical for harmonizing safety labeling across markets.
keyRequirements: Frequency conventions (very common to very rare)
regulations.notes: International - Guidance on preparing Company Core Safety Information (CCSI) and Core Data Sheet (CDS).

regulations.title: Benefit-Risk Balance for Marketed Drugs: Evaluating Safety Signals
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive framework for benefit-risk evaluation in context of emerging safety signals. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) Signal evaluation methodology; (2) Benefit assessment in therapeutic context; (3) Risk characterization and quantification; (4) Benefit-risk integration framework; (5) Decision-making approaches; (6) Stakeholder communication. FOUNDATIONAL CONCEPTS: Introduced structured approach to benefit-risk that influenced ICH E2C(R2) PBRER benefit-risk section, EMA benefit-risk methodology, and FDA benefit-risk framework. Basis for CIOMS IX (risk minimisation). Remains relevant for regulatory decision-making on marketed products.
regulations.notes: International - Framework for evaluating benefit-risk balance when safety signals emerge for marketed drugs.

regulations.title: Development Safety Update Report (DSUR): Harmonizing the Format and Content for Periodic Safety Reporting During Clinical Trials
regulations.summaryDescription: Established harmonized format for periodic safety reporting during clinical development, directly adopted as ICH E2F. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) Development International Birth Date (DIBD) concept; (2) Annual reporting cycle; (3) Comprehensive cumulative safety analysis; (4) Reference Safety Information updates; (5) Overall safety evaluation; (6) Benefit-risk conclusions for continuing development. SECTIONS: Executive summary, Introduction, Worldwide marketing approval status, Update on regulatory actions, Study information, Safety reference information, Findings from clinical trials, Safety findings from other sources, Nonclinical findings, Late-breaking information, Overall safety assessment, Benefit-risk evaluation, Summary of important risks, Conclusions.
regulations.notes: International - Foundation for ICH E2F DSUR. Harmonized format for annual safety reporting during clinical development.

regulations.title: Drug-Induced Liver Injury: A CIOMS Working Group Report
regulations.summaryDescription: Definitive guidance on drug-induced liver injury assessment. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) DILI phenotypes - hepatocellular, cholestatic, mixed; (2) Causality assessment including updated RUCAM; (3) Hy's Law and modified criteria; (4) Clinical trial DILI monitoring; (5) Post-marketing DILI signal detection; (6) Biomarkers for DILI prediction and detection; (7) Patient and genetic risk factors. CRITICAL CONTENT: Updated RUCAM (Roussel Uclaf Causality Assessment Method) with improved inter-rater reliability. Hy's Law criteria (ALT >3x ULN + TBL >2x ULN without ALP elevation) as predictor of severe DILI. Guidance on stopping rules and rechallenge. Essential for hepatotoxicity signal management.
keyRequirements: Stopping rules (ALT >8x, ALT >5x with symptoms, ALT >3x + TBL >2x)
Biomarker guidance (ALT, AST, ALP, TBL, INR)
regulations.notes: International - Comprehensive guidance on assessment, diagnosis, and management of drug-induced liver injury (DILI).

regulations.title: Real-World Data and Real-World Evidence in Drug Development and Pharmacovigilance
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive guidance on RWD/RWE application in drug development and pharmacovigilance. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) RWD sources - EHRs, claims, registries, wearables, patient surveys; (2) Data quality and fitness for purpose; (3) Study designs - cohort, case-control, self-controlled; (4) Confounding and bias mitigation; (5) Regulatory applications; (6) PV-specific applications. PV APPLICATIONS: Signal detection and evaluation using RWD; Safety study design; Effectiveness of risk minimisation measures; Long-term safety monitoring; Comparative safety studies; Special populations safety; Drug utilization studies. Addresses integration with spontaneous reporting and clinical trial data.
regulations.notes: International - Framework for using real-world data and real-world evidence in drug development and pharmacovigilance.

regulations.title: Definition and Application of Terms for Vaccine Pharmacovigilance
regulations.summaryDescription: Foundational document establishing harmonized terminology for vaccine pharmacovigilance. KEY CONTENT: (1) Standardized definitions for AEFI (Adverse Event Following Immunization); (2) Causality assessment framework for vaccines; (3) Classification of AEFIs - vaccine product-related, vaccine quality defect-related, immunization error-related, immunization anxiety-related, coincidental; (4) Brighton Collaboration case definitions integration; (5) Vaccination failure definitions. UNIQUE VACCINE CONSIDERATIONS: Healthy population (often children); Public health vs individual benefit; Herd immunity concepts; Lot/batch-specific issues; Cold chain impacts. Essential reference for all vaccine safety activities globally.
regulations.notes: International - Joint CIOMS/WHO report establishing standardized definitions and terminology for vaccine pharmacovigilance.

regulations.title: Guide to Active Vaccine Safety Surveillance
regulations.summaryDescription: Practical guidance for implementing Active Vaccine Safety Surveillance (AVSS) systems. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) Six-step algorithm for determining AVSS need; (2) Stakeholder responsibilities - NRAs, NIPs, MAHs, WHO; (3) Study design options - cohort, case-control, self-controlled case series; (4) Data sources and collection methods; (5) Resource-limited country considerations; (6) Regional and international collaboration. AVSS TRIGGERS: New vaccine introduction; Signal from passive surveillance; Knowledge gaps about known risks; Special populations; New indications. Includes case studies and vaccine information source lists. Critical for post-licensure vaccine safety evaluation.
regulations.notes: International - Guidance on implementing active vaccine safety surveillance, particularly for resource-limited countries.

regulations.title: Guide to Vaccine Safety Communication
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive guidance on vaccine safety communication for regulatory authorities. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) Regulatory role distinct from public health authorities; (2) Strategic communication approach; (3) System components - defined functions, skilled personnel, stakeholder collaboration; (4) Vaccine Safety Communication Plans (VacSCPs); (5) Crisis communication principles; (6) Addressing vaccine hesitancy. COMMUNICATION PRINCIPLES: Transparency about assessments; Proactive vs reactive communication; Audience-appropriate messaging; Coordination with public health bodies; Social media considerations. Includes practical VacSCP template. Addresses informed choice and vaccine confidence.
regulations.notes: International - Guidance for regulatory authorities on vaccine safety communication in context of vaccine hesitancy.

regulations.title: Development and Rational Use of Standardised MedDRA Queries (SMQs) - Second Edition
regulations.summaryDescription: Definitive guide on Standardised MedDRA Queries for safety data retrieval. KEY CONTENT: (1) SMQ structure - narrow vs broad scope; (2) Hierarchical SMQs and sub-SMQs; (3) Algorithmic searches; (4) Development and testing methodology; (5) Implementation guidance; (6) Use in signal detection and evaluation. SMQ APPLICATIONS: Case retrieval from safety databases; Signal detection screening; Aggregate safety analysis; PBRER/PSUR preparation; Regulatory submissions; Literature monitoring. Over 100 SMQs covering major safety topics. Maintained by MSSO in synchrony with MedDRA versions. Essential tool for consistent safety data analysis across organizations.
regulations.notes: International - Comprehensive guidance on SMQ development, structure, and appropriate use in safety surveillance.

regulations.title: Benefit-Risk Balance for Medicinal Products
regulations.summaryDescription: Major update to benefit-risk assessment methodology, superseding CIOMS IV (1998). KEY ELEMENTS: (1) Structured B-R assessment framework requirement; (2) Quantitative analysis methods to support decisions; (3) Lifecycle approach - pre-approval through post-marketing; (4) Stakeholder perspectives including patients; (5) Uncertainty characterization; (6) Decision-making frameworks. MODERN APPROACHES: Effects tables; Multi-criteria decision analysis (MCDA); Benefit-Risk Action Team (BRAT) framework; Quantitative benefit-risk modeling; Visual presentations. Addresses both initial approval decisions and post-marketing reassessments when new safety information emerges. Incorporates patient preference data and real-world evidence.
regulations.notes: International - Comprehensive update on benefit-risk evaluation methodology, superseding CIOMS IV (1998).

regulations.title: Pharmacoepidemiology for Public Health
regulations.summaryDescription: Active Working Group addressing pharmacoepidemiology applications for public health. ANTICIPATED SCOPE: (1) Study design for public health questions; (2) Data sources - registries, claims, EHRs; (3) Causal inference methods; (4) Signal evaluation and validation; (5) Benefit-risk at population level; (6) Resource-limited settings considerations. WORKING GROUP STATUS: 5th meeting December 2024 (Geneva); 6th meeting October 2025 (Geneva). Expected to provide guidance on using pharmacoepidemiologic methods to support population-level safety decisions and public health policy. Complements CIOMS XIII (RWD/RWE) with public health focus.
regulations.notes: International - Active CIOMS Working Group on pharmacoepidemiology methods for public health decision-making.

regulations.title: Development Safety Update Report (DSUR) - Update
regulations.summaryDescription: Active Working Group updating DSUR guidance to reflect modern clinical development. ANTICIPATED UPDATES: (1) Adaptive and platform trial considerations; (2) Real-world data integration; (3) Decentralized trial safety data; (4) Digital health technologies; (5) Patient-reported outcomes; (6) Streamlined reporting for established products. CONTEXT: Original CIOMS VII (2006) adopted as ICH E2F (2010). Clinical development landscape has evolved significantly. New trial designs (adaptive, basket, umbrella, platform) create unique DSUR challenges. May result in updated ICH E2F or supplemental guidance.
regulations.notes: International - Active CIOMS Working Group updating DSUR guidance (CIOMS VII/ICH E2F).

regulations.title: Long-term Safety of Medicinal Products
regulations.summaryDescription: New Working Group addressing critical gap in long-term safety assessment. ANTICIPATED SCOPE: (1) Methodologies for detecting delayed/cumulative toxicity; (2) Long-term safety study design; (3) Registry-based long-term follow-up; (4) Carcinogenicity and reproductive effects surveillance; (5) Chronic use safety assessment; (6) Pediatric long-term developmental effects. RELEVANCE: Many safety issues emerge only after extended exposure or latency periods. Current PV systems may not optimally detect these signals. Will address chronic disease treatments, preventive therapies, and products used in children where developmental effects may take years to manifest.
regulations.notes: International - New CIOMS Working Group addressing long-term safety monitoring and assessment of medicinal products.

regulations.title: Guidance on the Need for Carcinogenicity Studies of Pharmaceuticals
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines criteria for determining need for carcinogenicity studies. KEY CRITERIA: (1) Duration of treatment - generally required if 6+ months continuous or intermittent for chronic/recurrent conditions; (2) Cause for concern - genotoxicity signals, structure-activity, preneoplastic lesions in repeat-dose; (3) Route and systemic exposure; (4) Patient population. EXEMPTIONS: Short-term use, life-threatening conditions without alternatives, genotoxic compounds (limit exposure). PV RELEVANCE: Carcinogenicity signals post-market; Long-term safety surveillance; Labeling implications; Risk communication for cancer risk.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Guidance on when carcinogenicity studies are needed based on drug characteristics and intended use.

regulations.title: Testing for Carcinogenicity of Pharmaceuticals
regulations.summaryDescription: Major revision providing flexible approaches to carcinogenicity assessment. KEY CHANGES IN R1: (1) Weight of evidence (WoE) approach may replace second species study; (2) Integrates genotoxicity, chronic toxicity, mechanistic data; (3) RasH2 transgenic mouse as alternative; (4) Reduces animal use where scientifically justified. STUDY OPTIONS: Two-year rat bioassay; RasH2 transgenic mouse (6-month); WoE assessment without second species. PV RELEVANCE: Carcinogenicity risk assessment; Post-market cancer signal investigation; Labeling for cancer risk; Long-term safety monitoring requirements.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - R1 revision providing flexibility in carcinogenicity testing approaches based on weight of evidence.

regulations.title: Dose Selection for Carcinogenicity Studies of Pharmaceuticals
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines approaches for dose selection in carcinogenicity studies. HIGH DOSE SELECTION: (1) MTD based on toxicity; (2) 25x clinical exposure (AUC); (3) Dose-limiting pharmacodynamic effects; (4) Saturation of absorption; (5) Maximum feasible dose (1500 mg/kg/day or 5% diet). R2 ADDITION: Added limit dose option when exposure-based approach supports. MID/LOW DOSE: Pharmacokinetics, dose-response, human relevance. PV RELEVANCE: Exposure margins for safety assessment; Dose-response for carcinogenicity signals; Relevance to human exposure levels.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Guidance on selecting appropriate doses for carcinogenicity studies including high dose selection criteria.

regulations.title: Guidance on Genotoxicity Testing and Data Interpretation for Pharmaceuticals Intended for Human Use
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines standard battery and follow-up for genotoxicity assessment. STANDARD BATTERY (Option 1): (1) Ames test (bacterial reverse mutation); (2) In vitro mammalian cell assay (chromosomal damage or gene mutation); (3) In vivo assay (typically micronucleus). OPTION 2: Ames + in vivo Comet + micronucleus (two tissues). INTERPRETATION: Positive in vitro may be followed up in vivo; Relevance assessment required. PV RELEVANCE: Genotoxicity signals critical for carcinogenicity risk; M7 impurity assessment links; Post-market mutagenicity concerns; Labeling for genotoxic potential.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Comprehensive guidance on genotoxicity testing strategy and interpretation of results.

regulations.title: Note for Guidance on Toxicokinetics: The Assessment of Systemic Exposure in Toxicity Studies
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines principles for toxicokinetics (TK) in nonclinical studies. KEY PURPOSES: (1) Establish exposure-toxicity relationships; (2) Support dose selection; (3) Assess species relevance; (4) Calculate safety margins. TK PARAMETERS: AUC, Cmax, time to steady-state, accumulation. STUDY TYPES: Integrate into toxicity studies or satellite groups. EXPOSURE MARGINS: Compare animal NOAEL exposure to human therapeutic exposure. PV RELEVANCE: Safety margins inform clinical dose selection; Species relevance for signal interpretation; Exposure-response for post-market signals.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Guidance on assessing systemic exposure in toxicity studies to support clinical safety assessment.

regulations.title: Pharmacokinetics: Guidance for Repeated Dose Tissue Distribution Studies
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines need and design for repeated dose tissue distribution studies. WHEN NEEDED: (1) Apparent long half-life based on PK; (2) Incomplete elimination in single-dose; (3) Unexpected organ toxicity; (4) Drug targets specific tissue. STUDY DESIGN: Multiple doses, tissue sampling at steady-state, measurement of parent and metabolites. TISSUES: Based on single-dose distribution and toxicity targets. PV RELEVANCE: Accumulation in tissues may explain delayed toxicity; Supports long-term safety assessment; Informs withdrawal period considerations.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Guidance on when repeated dose tissue distribution studies are needed and study design.

regulations.title: Duration of Chronic Toxicity Testing in Animals (Rodent and Non-Rodent Toxicity Testing)
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines appropriate duration of repeat-dose toxicity studies. KEY PRINCIPLES: (1) Duration should equal or exceed clinical use; (2) Maximum 6 months rodent, 9 months non-rodent for marketing; (3) Shorter studies for limited clinical duration. DURATION TABLE: 2 weeks clinical = 1 month tox; up to 1 month = 3 months; 1-3 months = 6 months; >3 months = 6 months rodent + 9 months non-rodent. PV RELEVANCE: Chronic toxicity informs long-term safety; NOAELs for safety margin calculation; Delayed toxicity detection; Post-market signal interpretation.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Defines duration of chronic toxicity studies based on intended clinical use duration.

regulations.title: Detection of Reproductive and Developmental Toxicity for Human Pharmaceuticals
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive guidance on reproductive toxicity assessment. STUDY TYPES: (1) Fertility and early embryonic development (FEED); (2) Embryo-fetal development (EFD); (3) Pre- and postnatal development (PPND). R3 UPDATES: Alternative approaches, integrated study designs, reduced animal use, endpoints for developmental neurotoxicity and immunotoxicity. STAGING: EFD before women of childbearing potential; FEED/PPND before Phase 3 or approval. PV RELEVANCE: Critical for pregnancy labeling; Post-market pregnancy registry data interpretation; Teratogenicity signals; Lactation considerations; Fertility effects.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - R3 revision modernizing reproductive and developmental toxicity testing approaches.

regulations.title: Immunotoxicity Studies for Human Pharmaceuticals
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines approach to immunotoxicity assessment. INITIAL ASSESSMENT: Standard toxicity study parameters - immune organs (thymus, spleen, lymph nodes), hematology (WBC, lymphocytes), globulins, immune-related infections/tumors. TRIGGERS FOR ADDITIONAL TESTING: Immunomodulatory pharmacology, class effects, structural alerts, findings in standard studies. ADDITIONAL STUDIES: T-cell dependent antibody response (TDAR), immunophenotyping, NK cell activity, host resistance, macrophage/neutrophil function. PV RELEVANCE: Immunosuppression signals (infections, malignancies); Immunostimulation (autoimmunity, hypersensitivity); Post-market immune-related adverse events.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Guidance on nonclinical immunotoxicity evaluation for pharmaceuticals.

regulations.title: Photosafety Evaluation of Pharmaceuticals
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines tiered approach to photosafety assessment. INITIAL ASSESSMENT: UV-Vis absorption spectrum; If absorbs 290-700nm, evaluate further. PHOTOCHEMICAL: Reactive oxygen species (ROS), photostability. IN VITRO: 3T3 NRU phototoxicity test (EU validated). IN VIVO: May not be needed if in vitro negative and no distribution to skin/eyes. CLINICAL: Clinical assessment if preclinical concern. PV RELEVANCE: Photosensitivity reactions post-market; Labeling for sun exposure precautions; Patient education requirements; Phototoxicity vs photoallergy distinction.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Guidance on photosafety evaluation strategy for pharmaceuticals.

regulations.title: Nonclinical Safety Testing in Support of Development of Paediatric Pharmaceuticals
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines when and how to conduct juvenile animal studies (JAS). WHEN NEEDED: Novel mechanism without adult data; Concern for developmental effects; Pediatric-specific indication. JAS DESIGN: Species selection (typically rodent), age at start, duration, endpoints (developmental, organ-specific). ENDPOINTS: CNS, skeletal, reproductive organ development, immune system maturation. ALTERNATIVES: Adult data extrapolation when appropriate. PV RELEVANCE: Pediatric-specific safety signals; Growth and development effects; Long-term follow-up needs; Pediatric labeling support; Post-market pediatric studies.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Guidance on juvenile animal studies and nonclinical approaches for pediatric drug development.

regulations.title: Nonclinical Biodistribution Considerations for Gene Therapy Products
regulations.summaryDescription: First ICH guideline specific to gene therapy biodistribution. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) Biodistribution study design - vector, transgene expression, integration; (2) Tissues of interest - target and non-target; (3) Shedding studies; (4) Germline transmission assessment; (5) Duration of follow-up. STUDY ENDPOINTS: Vector DNA by qPCR, transgene expression (mRNA, protein), histopathology correlation. SUPPORTS: Clinical dosing, patient monitoring, long-term follow-up design. PV RELEVANCE: Gene therapy long-term safety; Delayed adverse events; Insertional mutagenesis risk; Secondary malignancy surveillance; Patient registries.
regulations.notes: Global (ICH regions) - Guidance on biodistribution studies for gene therapy products to support clinical development and safety.

regulations.title: Drug Administration Law of the People's Republic of China (2019 Revision)
regulations.summaryDescription: Foundational law for drug regulation in China. KEY PV PROVISIONS: (1) Marketing Authorization Holder (MAH) system - full lifecycle responsibility; (2) ADR reporting obligations; (3) Risk management requirements; (4) Recall authority; (5) Criminal liability for safety violations. MAH RESPONSIBILITIES: Safety monitoring throughout product lifecycle; ADR collection and reporting; Risk investigation and control measures; Annual safety reports. 2019 REVISION: Major modernization establishing MAH system, enhancing PV requirements, increasing penalties. World's 2nd largest pharmaceutical market.
regulations.notes: China - Primary legislation governing drug administration including pharmacovigilance requirements.

regulations.title: Measures for the Reporting and Monitoring of Adverse Drug Reactions
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines ADR reporting and monitoring requirements. REPORTING TIMELINES: Serious/unexpected ADRs - 15 days; Deaths - immediate notification; Periodic reports - annual. REPORTING SOURCES: MAHs, healthcare institutions, distributors. NATIONAL ADR MONITORING CENTER: Central collection and analysis. PROVINCIAL CENTERS: Regional monitoring network. KEY REQUIREMENTS: (1) All known and unknown ADRs; (2) Domestic and foreign reports; (3) Signal detection and assessment; (4) Risk control measures. Establishes China's pharmacovigilance infrastructure.
regulations.notes: China - Core regulation defining ADR reporting requirements, timelines, and monitoring system.

regulations.title: Guidelines for Good Pharmacovigilance Practice
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines GVP requirements for MAHs in China. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) PV system organization - qualified person, resources; (2) PV system master file requirements; (3) SOPs for all PV activities; (4) Training requirements; (5) Quality management system; (6) Outsourcing oversight. PV ACTIVITIES: ADR collection, assessment, reporting; Signal detection and management; Risk management; Periodic safety evaluation; Safety communication. Based on ICH/EU GVP principles adapted for China. MAHs must establish comprehensive PV systems.
regulations.notes: China - Guidelines establishing GVP standards for MAHs including PV system requirements.

regulations.title: Requirements for Periodic Safety Update Reports in China
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines PSUR requirements for China market. KEY REQUIREMENTS: (1) Annual PSURs for first 5 years post-approval; (2) Every 5 years thereafter; (3) ICH E2C(R2) PBRER format accepted with China addendum; (4) China-specific safety data required. CONTENT: Global safety data plus China-specific analysis; Chinese patient exposure; China signal discussion; Benefit-risk for Chinese population. SUBMISSION: Electronic submission to NMPA. New drugs may have more frequent requirements during monitoring period.
regulations.notes: China - Requirements for periodic safety update report submission to NMPA.

regulations.title: Resolution RDC 406/2020 - Good Pharmacovigilance Practices
regulations.summaryDescription: Core Brazilian pharmacovigilance regulation. KEY REQUIREMENTS: (1) Establishment of pharmacovigilance system; (2) ADR reporting - serious 15 days, periodic aggregate; (3) Risk management plans; (4) Periodic safety reports (PSURs); (5) Signal management; (6) Post-market safety studies. MAH OBLIGATIONS: Designate responsible person; Maintain PV system; Report all ADRs; Submit PSURs; Conduct benefit-risk assessments. ANVISA ROLE: National ADR database (Notivisa); Signal detection; Regulatory actions. Brazil is largest LATAM pharmaceutical market.
regulations.notes: Brazil - Primary pharmacovigilance regulation establishing GVP requirements and ADR reporting obligations.

regulations.title: Resolution RDC 753/2022 - Periodic Safety Update Reports
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines PSUR requirements for Brazilian market. KEY REQUIREMENTS: (1) PBRER format per ICH E2C(R2) accepted; (2) Submission frequency based on approval date; (3) Brazil-specific addendum required; (4) Electronic submission via ANVISA portal. FREQUENCY: First 2 years - every 6 months; Years 3-5 - annual; After 5 years - every 3 years or upon request. CONTENT: Global PBRER plus Brazil-specific exposure, signal discussion, local actions. Aligns Brazil with international PSUR standards.
regulations.notes: Brazil - Requirements for PSUR/PBRER submission to ANVISA.

regulations.title: Resolution RDC 657/2022 - Risk Management Plans
regulations.summaryDescription: Defines RMP requirements for Brazil. KEY ELEMENTS: (1) Safety specification; (2) Pharmacovigilance plan; (3) Risk minimization measures; (4) Effectiveness evaluation. RMP REQUIRED FOR: New active substances; Biosimilars; Generics with safety concerns; Significant safety updates. FORMAT: EU RMP format accepted with Brazil adaptations. UPDATES: Triggered by new safety information, regulatory request, or periodic review. Aligns Brazil with ICH E2E and EU RMP concepts.
regulations.notes: Brazil - Requirements for Risk Management Plans and risk minimization measures.

regulations.officialIdentifier: New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules 2019
regulations.title: New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules, 2019
regulations.summaryDescription: Comprehensive rules for drug development and post-market surveillance. KEY PV PROVISIONS: (1) ADR reporting requirements; (2) Serious Adverse Event (SAE) reporting from clinical trials; (3) Post-marketing surveillance; (4) Compensation for trial injury. TIMELINES: SAEs from trials - 24 hours notification, 14 days detailed report; Post-market serious ADRs - 15 days. NEW DRUG MONITORING: Additional PV requirements for first 4 years post-approval. Major reform streamlining Indian drug regulation and enhancing safety monitoring.
regulations.notes: India - Primary rules governing new drugs, clinical trials, and post-market safety requirements.

regulations.title: Good Clinical Practice - Complete Revision (Step 4 Final)
regulations.summaryDescription: ICH E6(R3) represents a paradigm shift from prescriptive rules to principle-based, risk-proportionate approaches. Introduces Quality by Design (QbD) as foundational principle, promoting critical thinking and fit-for-purpose solutions. Supports innovative trial designs including decentralized and hybrid trials. Reorganized structure: Introduction, GCP Principles, Annex 1 (IRB/IEC, Investigator, Sponsor responsibilities, Data Governance), three Appendices (IB, Protocol, Essential Records), and Glossary.
keyRequirements:
1. QUALITY BY DESIGN (QbD):
   - Critical process factors identification
   - Risk-proportionate approach
   - Fit-for-purpose solutions
   - Continuous quality improvement

2. RISK-BASED QUALITY MANAGEMENT:
   - Identify critical data and processes
   - Proportionate risk controls
   - Ongoing risk assessment
   - Quality tolerance limits

3. DATA GOVERNANCE (NEW):
   - Data governance plan required
   - Data integrity throughout lifecycle
   - Electronic systems validation
   - Audit trail requirements

4. DECENTRALIZED/HYBRID TRIALS:
   - Explicit support for remote elements
   - Technology-enabled visits
   - Central monitoring emphasis
   - Direct-to-participant drug shipment

5. PARTICIPANT SAFETY:
   - Enhanced safety reporting
   - Risk-benefit reassessment
   - Safety monitoring committees
   - Participant-centric approaches

6. ESSENTIAL RECORDS:
   - Revised essential documents list
   - Fit-for-purpose documentation
   - Electronic records acceptable
   - Traceability requirements
regulations.notes: ICH - Complete revision of Good Clinical Practice guideline. Step 4 Final signed 6 January 2025. Coming into effect 23 July 2025 (EMA). Paradigm shift with Quality by Design (QbD), risk-based quality management, and decentralized trial support.
